Kikuyu: No Ngai akiumĩrĩra Labani ũcio Mũsuriata kĩroto-inĩ, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Menya wĩre Jakubu ũndũ o na ũrĩkũ, ũrĩ mwega kana ũrĩ mũũru.”
English: God came to Laban the Syrian in a dream of the night, and said to him, “Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jakubu aambĩte hema yake kũu bũrũri wa irĩma wa Gileadi rĩrĩa Labani aamũkinyĩrire, nake Labani na andũ a nyũmba yao makĩamba hema ciao kuo.
English: Laban caught up with Jacob. Now Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountain, and Laban with his relatives encamped in the mountain of Gilead.

Kikuyu: Nake Labani akĩũria Jakubu atĩrĩ, “Nĩ atĩa ũũ wĩkĩte? Nĩũũheenetie, na ũgathiĩ na airĩtu akwa ta andũ matahĩtwo mbaara-inĩ.
English: Laban said to Jacob, “What have you done, that you have deceived me, and carried away my daughters like captives of the sword?

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ gĩatũmire ũre na hitho, na ũũheenie? Waregire kũnjĩĩra nĩkĩ, nĩguo ngumagarie na gĩkeno na gũkũinĩra nyĩmbo na ihembe na inanda cia mũgeeto?
English: Why did you flee secretly, and deceive me, and didn’t tell me, that I might have sent you away with mirth and with songs, with tambourine and with harp;

Kikuyu: O na ndwarekire mumunye ciana cia ciana ciakwa, kana njugĩre airĩtu akwa ũhoro. Nĩwĩkĩte ũndũ wa ũrimũ.
English: and didn’t allow me to kiss my sons and my daughters? Now you have done foolishly.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na hinya wa gũkũgera ngero; no rĩrĩ, ũtukũ wa ira Ngai wa thoguo aranjĩĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Menya wĩre Jakubu ũndũ, ũrĩ mwega kana ũrĩ mũũru.’
English: It is in the power of my hand to hurt you, but the God of your father spoke to me last night, saying, ‘Be careful that you don’t speak to Jacob either good or bad.’

Kikuyu: Rĩu-rĩ, nĩumagarĩte nĩ ũndũ nĩũrerirĩria gũcooka nyũmba-inĩ ya thoguo. No rĩrĩ, ũrakĩiyire ngai ciakwa nĩkĩ?”
English: Now, you want to be gone, because you greatly longed for your father’s house, but why have you stolen my gods?”

Kikuyu: Jakubu agĩcookeria Labani atĩrĩ, “Nĩndetigĩrire tondũ ndeciiririe wahota kũndunya airĩtu aku na hinya.
English: Jacob answered Laban, “Because I was afraid, for I said, ‘Lest you should take your daughters from me by force.’

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩ, ũrĩa ũgũkora arĩ na ngai ciaku-rĩ, ndegũtũũra muoyo. Tuĩria wone kana kũrĩ kĩndũ gĩaku ndĩ nakĩo andũ aya aitũ marĩ ho; na wakĩona-rĩ, ũkĩoe.” No Jakubu ndooĩ atĩ Rakeli nĩaiyĩte ngai icio.
English: Anyone you find your gods with shall not live. Before our relatives, discern what is yours with me, and take it.” For Jacob didn’t know that Rachel had stolen them.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Labani agĩtoonya hema ya Jakubu, na hema ya Lea, o na hema ya ndungata iria igĩrĩ cia andũ-a-nja, no ndaigana kuona kĩndũ. Oima hema ya Lea, agĩtoonya ya Rakeli.
English: Laban went into Jacob’s tent, into Leah’s tent, and into the tent of the two female servants; but he didn’t find them. He went out of Leah’s tent, and entered into Rachel’s tent.

Kikuyu: Rakeli oete ngai icio cia mĩhianano na agacikia matandĩko-inĩ ma ngamĩĩra thĩinĩ na akamaikarĩra. Labani akiururia kĩrĩa gĩothe kĩarĩ hema-inĩ ĩyo na ndaigana kuona kĩndũ.
English: Now Rachel had taken the teraphim, put them in the camel’s saddle, and sat on them. Laban felt around all the tent, but didn’t find them.

Kikuyu: Nake Rakeli akĩĩra ithe atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa, ndũkandakarĩre nĩ ũndũ ndinakũrũgamĩra; nĩgũkorwo nĩ maũndũ ma andũ-a-nja ndĩ namo.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akiururia no ndaigana kuona ngai icio.
English: She said to her father, “Don’t let my lord be angry that I can’t rise up before you; for I’m having my period.” He searched, but didn’t find the teraphim.

Kikuyu: Jakubu agĩkĩrakara na agĩtetia Labani, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ihĩtia rĩakwa nĩ rĩrĩkũ? Nĩ rĩhia rĩrĩkũ njĩkĩte rĩtũmĩte ũnyingatithie ũguo?
English: Jacob was angry, and argued with Laban. Jacob answered Laban, “What is my trespass? What is my sin, that you have hotly pursued me?

Kikuyu: Rĩu tondũ nĩwoiruria indo ciakwa ciothe-rĩ, nĩ kĩĩ wona kĩa indo cia gwaku mũciĩ? Kĩige haha mbere ya andũ aya anyu na mbere ya andũ akwa nĩguo matũtuithanie ciira.
English: Now that you have felt around in all my stuff, what have you found of all your household stuff? Set it here before my relatives and your relatives, that they may judge between us two.

Kikuyu: “Ngũrutĩire wĩra mĩaka mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ. Ngʼondu ciaku na mbũri ciaku itirĩ ciahuna, na ndirĩ ndarĩa ndũrũme cia mahiũ maku.
English: “These twenty years I have been with you. Your ewes and your female goats have not cast their young, and I haven’t eaten the rams of your flocks.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ ndirĩ ndakũrehera iria ciatambuuragwo nĩ nyamũ cia gĩthaka; no nĩ niĩ ndaarĩhaga ngathiĩ hathara. Ningĩ-rĩ, nĩwandĩhagia ĩrĩa yothe yaiywo kũrĩ mũthenya kana kũrĩ ũtukũ.
English: That which was torn of animals, I didn’t bring to you. I bore its loss. Of my hand you required it, whether stolen by day or stolen by night.

Kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo ndũũrĩte: Ndaarugagwo nĩ ũrugarĩ wa mũthenya na ngarĩĩo nĩ heho ya ũtukũ, naguo toro ũkĩnjũrĩra.
English: This was my situation: in the day the drought consumed me, and the frost by night; and my sleep fled from my eyes.

Kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo ndũũrĩte gwaku mũciĩ handũ-inĩ ha mĩaka ĩyo mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ. Ndakũrutĩire wĩra mĩaka ikũmi na ĩna nĩ ũndũ wa airĩtu aku eerĩ, na mĩaka ĩtandatũ nĩ ũndũ wa mahiũ maku; no rĩrĩ, wagarũrire mũcaara wakwa maita ikũmi.
English: These twenty years I have been in your house. I served you fourteen years for your two daughters, and six years for your flock, and you have changed my wages ten times.

Kikuyu: Korwo Ngai wa baba, o we Ngai wa Iburahĩmu na nĩwe wĩtigĩrĩtwo nĩ Isaaka, ndaraakoragwo hamwe na niĩ-rĩ, ti-itherũ ũngĩranyumagaririe moko matheri. No Ngai nĩonete gũthĩĩnĩka gwakwa na wĩra wa moko makwa, na nĩkĩo arakũrũithirie ũtukũ wa ira.”
English: Unless the God of my father, the God of Abraham, and the fear of Isaac, had been with me, surely now you would have sent me away empty. God has seen my affliction and the labor of my hands, and rebuked you last night.”

Kikuyu: Nake Labani agĩcookeria Jakubu, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Atumia aya aku nĩ airĩtu akwa, ciana ici o nacio nĩ ciana ciakwa, namo mahiũ maya-rĩ, no mahiũ makwa. Kĩrĩa gĩothe ũroona nĩ gĩakwa. No rĩrĩ, nĩ atĩa ingĩhota gwĩka airĩtu aya akwa kana njĩke ciana ici maciarĩte ũmũthĩ?
English: Laban answered Jacob, “The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, the flocks are my flocks, and all that you see is mine! What can I do today to these my daughters, or to their children whom they have borne?

Kikuyu: Rĩu gĩũke, reke tũgĩe na kĩrĩkanĩro niĩ nawe, na ũreke gĩtuĩke mũira gatagatĩ gaitũ.”
English: Now come, let’s make a covenant, you and I. Let it be for a witness between me and you.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jakubu akĩoya ihiga na akĩrĩhaanda ta gĩtugĩ.
English: Jacob took a stone, and set it up for a pillar.

Kikuyu: Akĩĩra andũ a nyũmba yake atĩrĩ, “Cookanĩrĩriai mahiga.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩũngania mahiga makĩmaiganĩrĩra hĩba, magĩcooka makĩrĩanĩra irio hau hakuhĩ na hĩba ĩyo.
English: Jacob said to his relatives, “Gather stones.” They took stones, and made a heap. They ate there by the heap.

Kikuyu: Labani akĩmĩĩta Jagari-Sahadutha, nake Jakubu akĩmĩĩta Galeedi.
English: Laban called it Jegar Sahadutha,‡ but Jacob called it Galeed.§

Kikuyu: Labani akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũmũthĩ hĩba ĩno nĩ mũira gatagatĩ gakwa nawe.” Kĩu nĩkĩo gĩatũmire hetwo Galeedi.
English: Laban said, “This heap is witness between me and you today.” Therefore it was named Galeed

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ hagĩĩtwo Mizipa, tondũ oigire atĩrĩ, “Jehova arotũmenyagĩrĩra rĩrĩa tũraihanĩrĩirie.
English: and Mizpah, for he said, “Yahweh watch between me and you, when we are absent one from another.

Kikuyu: Wee ũngĩgaathĩĩnia airĩtu akwa kana ũmahikĩrĩrie atumia angĩ, o na hangĩgakorwo hatarĩ mũndũ ũngĩ-rĩ, ririkana atĩ Ngai nĩwe mũira gatagatĩ gakwa nawe.”
English: If you afflict my daughters, or if you take wives in addition to my daughters, no man is with us; behold, God is witness between me and you.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Labani akĩĩra Jakubu atĩrĩ, “Ĩno nĩ hĩba, na gĩkĩ nĩ gĩtugĩ kĩrĩa ndahaanda gatagatĩ gaku na niĩ.
English: Laban said to Jacob, “See this heap, and see the pillar, which I have set between me and you.

Kikuyu: Hĩba ĩno nĩ mũira, na gĩtugĩ gĩkĩ nĩ mũira, atĩ ndigakĩra hĩba ĩno njũke mwena waku ngũgere ngero, na atĩ nawe ndũgakĩra hĩba ĩno, kana ũkĩre gĩtugĩ gĩkĩ ũũke mwena wakwa ũngere ngero.
English: May this heap be a witness, and the pillar be a witness, that I will not pass over this heap to you, and that you will not pass over this heap and this pillar to me, for harm.

Kikuyu: Ngai wa Iburahĩmu, na Ngai wa Nahoru,* o we Ngai wa ithe wao-rĩ, arotũtuithania ciira.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jakubu akĩĩhĩta na mwĩhĩtwa rĩĩtwa-inĩ rĩa ũcio Wĩtigĩrĩtwo nĩ ithe Isaaka.
English: The God of Abraham, and the God of Nahor, the God of their father, judge between us.” Then Jacob swore by the fear of his father, Isaac.

Kikuyu: Jakubu akĩruta igongona kũu bũrũri-inĩ ũcio wa irĩma, na agĩĩta andũ a nyũmba yake marĩĩanĩre. Na marĩkia kũrĩa-rĩ, makĩraara o kũu.
English: Jacob offered a sacrifice in the mountain, and called his relatives to eat bread. They ate bread, and stayed all night in the mountain.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ rũciinĩ tene, Labani akĩmumunya ciana cia ciana ciake o na airĩtu ake, akĩmoigĩra ũhoro na akĩmarathima. Agĩcooka akĩmatiga agĩcooka gwake mũciĩ. 32
English: Early in the morning, Laban rose up, and kissed his sons and his daughters, and blessed them. Laban departed and returned to his place. 32

Kikuyu: Jakubu nĩathiire na mbere na rũgendo rwake, na agĩtũngwo nĩ araika a Ngai.
English: Jacob went on his way, and the angels of God met him.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Jakubu aamoonire, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ĩno nĩ mbũtũ ya Ngai!” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩĩta handũ hau Mahanaimu.
English: When he saw them, Jacob said, “This is God’s army.” He called the name of that place Mahanaim.†

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jakubu agĩtũma andũ mathiĩ mbere yake kũrĩ mũrũ wa nyina Esaũ kũu Seiru, bũrũri-inĩ wa Edomu.
English: Jacob sent messengers in front of him to Esau, his brother, to the land of Seir, the field of Edom.

Kikuyu: Akĩmataara, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũũ nĩguo mũgũthiĩ kwĩra mwathi wakwa Esaũ: ‘Ndungata yaku Jakubu ĩkuuga atĩrĩ, ndĩraikaraga na Labani, na ngoretwo kuo nginya rĩu.
English: He commanded them, saying, “This is what you shall tell my lord, Esau: ‘This is what your servant, Jacob, says. I have lived as a foreigner with Laban, and stayed until now.

Kikuyu: Ndĩ na ngʼombe na ndigiri, na ngʼondu na mbũri, ndungata cia arũme na cia andũ-a-nja. Na rĩu ndagũtũmĩra ndũmĩrĩri, we mwathi wakwa, nĩgeetha njĩtĩkĩrĩke maitho-inĩ maku.’ ”
English: I have cattle, donkeys, flocks, male servants, and female servants. I have sent to tell my lord, that I may find favor in your sight.’ ”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa atũmwo maacookire kũrĩ Jakubu makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũrathiire kũrĩ mũrũ wa nyũkwa Esaũ, na rĩu nĩarooka gũgũtũnga, na arĩ na andũ magana mana.”
English: The messengers returned to Jacob, saying, “We came to your brother Esau. He is coming to meet you, and four hundred men are with him.”

Kikuyu: Jakubu akĩmaka mũno na akĩnyamarĩka, akĩgayania andũ arĩa aarĩ nao ikundi igĩrĩ, o na ndũũru cia mbũri na ndũũru cia ngʼombe o na ngamĩĩra nacio.
English: Then Jacob was greatly afraid and was distressed. He divided the people who were with him, along with the flocks, the herds, and the camels, into two companies.

Kikuyu: Agĩĩciiria atĩrĩ, “Esaũ angĩũka atharĩkĩre gĩkundi kĩmwe-rĩ, gĩkundi kĩu kĩngĩ no kĩũre.”
English: He said, “If Esau comes to the one company, and strikes it, then the company which is left will escape.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jakubu akĩhooya, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Wee Ngai wa baba Iburahĩmu, o Wee Ngai wa baba Isaaka, Wee Jehova ũrĩa wanjĩĩrire atĩrĩ, ‘Cooka bũrũri waku na wa andũ anyu, na nĩngatũma ũgaacĩre,’
English: Jacob said, “God of my father Abraham, and God of my father Isaac, Yahweh, who said to me, ‘Return to your country, and to your relatives, and I will do you good,’

Kikuyu: ndirĩ mwagĩrĩru wa kuonio ũtugi ũyũ wothe na wĩhokeku ũrĩa ũnyonetie niĩ ndungata yaku. Ngĩringa Jorodani-rĩ, ndaarĩ o na rũthanju rwakwa rwiki, no rĩu nduĩkĩte ikundi igĩrĩ.
English: I am not worthy of the least of all the loving kindnesses, and of all the truth, which you have shown to your servant; for with just my staff I crossed over this Jordan; and now I have become two companies.

Kikuyu: Ndagũthaitha honokia kuuma moko-inĩ ma mũrũ wa maitũ Esaũ, nĩgũkorwo nĩndĩretigĩra ndagoke gũũtharĩkĩra, niĩ hamwe na ciana ici, na manyina maacio.
English: Please deliver me from the hand of my brother, from the hand of Esau; for I fear him, lest he come and strike me and the mothers with the children.

Kikuyu: No nĩ uugĩte atĩrĩ, ‘Ti-itherũ nĩngatũma ũgaacĩre na ndũme njiaro ciaku cingĩhe ta mũthanga wa iria-inĩ, ũrĩa ũtangĩtarĩka.’ ”
English: You said, ‘I will surely do you good, and make your offspring as the sand of the sea, which can’t be counted because there are so many.’ ”

Kikuyu: Akĩraara hau ũtukũ ũcio, na kuuma kũrĩ kĩrĩa aarĩ nakĩo, agĩthuurĩra mũrũ wa nyina Esaũ kĩheo gĩkĩ;
English: He stayed there that night, and took from that which he had with him a present for Esau, his brother:

Kikuyu: mbũri magana meerĩ cia mĩgoma na thenge mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ, ngʼondu magana meerĩ na ndũrũme mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ,
English: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams,

Kikuyu: ngamĩĩra mĩrongo ĩtatũ cia mĩgoma na njaũ ciacio, ngʼombe mĩrongo ĩna na ndegwa ikũmi, na ndigiri mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ cia mĩgoma na ikũmi cia njamba.
English: thirty milk camels and their colts, forty cows, ten bulls, twenty female donkeys and ten foals.

Kikuyu: Agĩcinengera ndungata ciake, o rũũru mwanya, na akĩĩra ndungata icio ciake atĩrĩ, “Thiiagai mbere yakwa, na mũtigithũkanie rũũru na rũũru rũrĩa rũngĩ.”
English: He delivered them into the hands of his servants, every herd by itself, and said to his servants, “Pass over before me, and put a space between herd and herd.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩtaara ũrĩa wamatongoretie, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rĩrĩa mũrũ wa maitũ Esaũ arĩgũtũnga na akũũrie atĩrĩ, ‘Wee ũrĩ waũ? Na wathiĩ kũ? Na ũhiũ ũyũ wothe ũrĩ mbere yaku nĩ waũ?’
English: He commanded the foremost, saying, “When Esau, my brother, meets you, and asks you, saying, ‘Whose are you? Where are you going? Whose are these before you?’

Kikuyu: Nawe nĩũrĩmũcookeria ũmwĩre atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ cia ndungata yaku Jakubu. Nĩ kĩheo gĩa kũhe mwathi wakwa Esaũ, na Jakubu arĩ thuutha witũ agĩũka.’ ”
English: Then you shall say, ‘They are your servant, Jacob’s. It is a present sent to my lord, Esau. Behold, he also is behind us.’ ”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtaara wa keerĩ na wa gatatũ, na arĩa othe marũmagĩrĩra ndũũru icio, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũguo nĩguo mũrĩĩraga Esaũ mwamũtũnga.
English: He commanded also the second, and the third, and all that followed the herds, saying, “This is how you shall speak to Esau, when you find him.

Kikuyu: Na mũtikanaage kũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Jakubu ndungata yaku arĩ thuutha witũ agĩũka.’ ” Tondũ eeciiririe atĩrĩ, “Ngũmũhooreria na iheo ici ngũtũma mbere yakwa; thuutha-inĩ ndamuona-rĩ, hihi ahota kũnyamũkĩra.”
English: You shall say, ‘Not only that, but behold, your servant, Jacob, is behind us.’ ” For, he said, “I will appease him with the present that goes before me, and afterward I will see his face. Perhaps he will accept me.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio iheo icio Jakubu aaheanĩte igĩthiiaga mbere yake, nowe akĩraara kũu kambĩ ũtukũ ũcio.
English: So the present passed over before him, and he himself stayed that night in the camp.

Kikuyu: Na ũtukũ o ro ũcio, Jakubu agĩũkĩra, akĩoya atumia ake eerĩ, na ndungata iria igĩrĩ cia andũ-a- nja, na ariũ ake ikũmi na ũmwe, na akĩmaringĩria iringĩro rĩa Jaboku.
English: He rose up that night, and took his two wives, and his two servants, and his eleven sons, and crossed over the ford of the Jabbok.

Kikuyu: Aarĩkia kũmaringia, akĩringia indo iria ciothe aarĩ nacio.
English: He took them, and sent them over the stream, and sent over that which he had.

Kikuyu: Nake Jakubu agĩtigwo arĩ o wiki, na hagĩũka mũndũ maagianire nake o nginya gũgĩthererũka.
English: Jacob was left alone, and wrestled with a man there until the breaking of the day.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mũndũ ũcio onire atĩ ndangĩmũhoota-rĩ, akĩmũhutia ihĩndĩ rĩa gĩtina gĩa kũgũrũ, nakuo kũgũrũ kwa Jakubu gũgĩthenyũkĩra hau gĩtina-inĩ, o makĩgianaga na mũndũ ũcio.
English: When he saw that he didn’t prevail against him, the man touched the hollow of his thigh, and the hollow of Jacob’s thigh was strained as he wrestled.

Kikuyu: Na mũndũ ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Reke ndĩthiĩre tondũ nĩ gwathererũka.” No Jakubu akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndingĩreka ũthiĩ ũtandathimĩte.”
English: The man said, “Let me go, for the day breaks.” Jacob said, “I won’t let you go unless you bless me.”

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ ũcio akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Wĩtagwo atĩa?” Jakubu akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Njĩtagwo Jakubu.”
English: He said to him, “What is your name?” He said, “Jacob”.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ mũndũ ũcio akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndũgũcooka gwĩtwo Jakubu, no ũrĩĩtagwo Isiraeli, tondũ nĩũgianĩte na Ngai o na andũ, na ũkahootana.”
English: He said, “Your name will no longer be called Jacob, but Israel; for you have fought with God and with men, and have prevailed.”

Kikuyu: Jakubu akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ndagũthaitha, njĩĩra rĩĩtwa rĩaku.” Nowe akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũranjũũria rĩĩtwa rĩakwa nĩkĩ?” Agĩcooka akĩmũrathima marĩ o hau.
English: Jacob asked him, “Please tell me your name.” He said, “Why is it that you ask what my name is?” So he blessed him there.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jakubu agĩĩta handũ hau Penieli, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Tondũ nĩnyonete Ngai ũthiũ kwa ũthiũ, na akahonokia muoyo wakwa.”
English: Jacob called the name of the place Peniel;‡ for he said, “I have seen God face to face, and my life is preserved.”

Kikuyu: Narĩo riũa rĩkĩmũrathĩra akĩhĩtũka hau Penieli, na nĩathuaga nĩ ũndũ nĩathenyũkĩtwo nĩ ihĩndĩ rĩa gĩtina gĩa kũgũrũ.
English: The sun rose on him as he passed over Peniel, and he limped because of his thigh.

Kikuyu: Nĩ tondũ ũcio, nginyagia ũmũthĩ ũyũ andũ a Isiraeli matirĩĩaga mũkiha ũrĩa ũnyiitanĩte na ihĩndĩ rĩa gĩtina gĩa kũgũrũ, tondũ ihĩndĩ rĩa gĩtina gĩa kũgũrũ kwa Jakubu rĩahutĩirio hakuhĩ na mũkiha ũcio. 33
English: Therefore the children of Israel don’t eat the sinew of the hip, which is on the hollow of the thigh, to this day, because he touched the hollow of Jacob’s thigh in the sinew of the hip. 33

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jakubu agĩtiira maitho, akĩona Esaũ agĩũka arĩ na andũ ake magana mana; nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩgayania ciana kũrĩ Lea na Rakeli na kũrĩ ndungata iria igĩrĩ cia andũ-a-nja.
English: Jacob lifted up his eyes, and looked, and, behold, Esau was coming, and with him four hundred men. He divided the children between Leah, Rachel, and the two servants.

Kikuyu: Akĩiga ndungata cia andũ-a-nja na ciana ciacio mbere, nake Lea na ciana ciake makĩrũmĩrĩra, nake Rakeli na Jusufu makĩrigia thuutha.
English: He put the servants and their children in front, Leah and her children after, and Rachel and Joseph at the rear.

Kikuyu: Nake we mwene akĩmatongoria, na rĩrĩa aakuhĩrĩirie mũrũ wa nyina, akĩinamĩrĩra, agĩturumithia ũthiũ wake thĩ maita mũgwanja.
English: He himself passed over in front of them, and bowed himself to the ground seven times, until he came near to his brother.

Kikuyu: No Esaũ akĩhanyũka agatũnge Jakubu na akĩmũnyiita, akĩmũhĩmbĩria, akĩmũmumunya. Nao makĩrĩranĩra.
English: Esau ran to meet him, embraced him, fell on his neck, kissed him, and they wept.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Esaũ agĩtiira maitho akĩona atumia na ciana. Akĩũria Jakubu atĩrĩ, “Andũ aya mũrĩ nao nĩ a ũ?” Jakubu agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ici nĩ ciana iria Ngai aheete ndungata yaku nĩ ũndũ wa ũtugi wake.”
English: He lifted up his eyes, and saw the women and the children; and said, “Who are these with you?” He said, “The children whom God has graciously given your servant.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ndungata icio cia andũ-a-nja na ciana ciacio igĩkuhĩrĩria na makĩinamĩrĩra.
English: Then the servants came near with their children, and they bowed themselves.

Kikuyu: Lea na ciana ciake makĩrũmĩrĩra, magĩũka na makĩinamĩrĩra. Marigĩrĩrio, Jusufu na Rakeli magĩũka, o nao makĩinamĩrĩra.
English: Leah also and her children came near, and bowed themselves. After them, Joseph came near with Rachel, and they bowed themselves.

Kikuyu: Esaũ akĩũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmi gĩa kũndũmĩra ikundi icio ndatũnga?” Nake Jakubu agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩgeetha njĩtĩkĩrĩke maitho-inĩ maku, wee mwathi wakwa.”
English: Esau said, “What do you mean by all this company which I met?” Jacob said, “To find favor in the sight of my lord.”

Kikuyu: No Esaũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa maitũ, niĩ ndĩ na indo cia kũnjigana. Ikara na icio ũrĩ nacio wee mwene.”
English: Esau said, “I have enough, my brother; let that which you have be yours.”

Kikuyu: Jakubu akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca, ndagũthaitha! Angĩkorwo nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩkĩte maitho-inĩ maku-rĩ, amũkĩra kĩheo gĩkĩ kuuma kũrĩ niĩ. Nĩ ũndũ kuona ũthiũ waku no ta kuona ũthiũ wa Ngai, tondũ nĩ wanyamũkĩra na gĩkeno.
English: Jacob said, “Please, no, if I have now found favor in your sight, then receive my present at my hand, because I have seen your face, as one sees the face of God, and you were pleased with me.

Kikuyu: Ndagũthaitha ĩtĩkĩra kĩheo kĩu ũreheirwo, tondũ Ngai nĩanjĩkĩte wega, na ndĩ na indo cia kũnjigana.” Na tondũ Jakubu nĩamũringĩrĩirie-rĩ, Esaũ agĩciĩtĩkĩra.
English: Please take the gift that I brought to you, because God has dealt graciously with me, and because I have enough.” He urged him, and he took it.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Esaũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Nĩtũthiĩ; nĩngũtwarana nawe.”
English: Esau said, “Let’s take our journey, and let’s go, and I will go before you.”

Kikuyu: No Jakubu akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathi wakwa nĩoĩ atĩ twana tũtũ tũtirĩ na hinya, na no nginya menyerere ngʼondu na ngʼombe iria irongithia. Ingĩtwarwo na ihenya o na mũthenya ũmwe, nyamũ ciothe no ikue.
English: Jacob said to him, “My lord knows that the children are tender, and that the flocks and herds with me have their young, and if they overdrive them one day, all the flocks will die.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mwathi wakwa nĩathiiage mbere ya ndungata yake, na niĩ thiĩ o kahora kũringana na mũthiĩre wa ikundi ici, o na wa ciana, nginya rĩrĩa ngaakinya kwa mwathi wakwa kũu Seiru.”
English: Please let my lord pass over before his servant, and I will lead on gently, according to the pace of the livestock that are before me and according to the pace of the children, until I come to my lord to Seir.”

Kikuyu: Esaũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “No kĩreke ngũtigĩre ndungata imwe ciakwa.” Jakubu akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũgwĩka ũguo nĩkĩ? Reke niĩ njĩtĩkĩrĩke maitho-inĩ ma mwathi wakwa.”
English: Esau said, “Let me now leave with you some of the people who are with me.” He said, “Why? Let me find favor in the sight of my lord.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Esaũ akĩhũndũka mũthenya o ro ũcio agĩthiĩ arorete Seiru.
English: So Esau returned that day on his way to Seir.

Kikuyu: Nowe Jakubu, agĩthiĩ Sukothu, kũrĩa eyakĩire nyũmba, na agĩthondeka ciugũ cia mahiũ make. Nĩkĩo handũ hau hetagwo Sukothu.
English: Jacob traveled to Succoth, built himself a house, and made shelters for his livestock. Therefore the name of the place is called Succoth.†

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa Jakubu kuuma Padani-Aramu, agĩkinya o wega itũũra-inĩ rĩrĩa inene rĩa Shekemu kũu Kaanani, na akĩamba hema hakuhĩ na itũũra rĩu inene.
English: Jacob came in peace to the city of Shechem, which is in the land of Canaan, when he came from Paddan Aram; and encamped before the city.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩgũra gĩcunjĩ kĩa mũgũnda na betha igana rĩmwe kuuma kũrĩ ariũ a Hamoru ithe wa Shekemu, harĩa aambire hema yake.
English: He bought the parcel of ground where he had spread his tent, at the hand of the children of Hamor, Shechem’s father, for one hundred pieces of money.

Kikuyu: Agĩaka kĩgongona handũ hau, na agĩgĩĩta Eli-Elohe-Isiraeli. 34
English: He erected an altar there, and called it El Elohe Israel.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, mũthenya ũmwe, Dina mũirĩtu ũrĩa Lea aaciarĩire Jakubu, nĩathiire gũceerera andũ-a-nja a bũrũri ũcio.
English: Dinah, the daughter of Leah, whom she bore to Jacob, went out to see the daughters of the land.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Shekemu mũrũ wa Hamoru ũrĩa Mũhivi, mũnene wa gĩcigo kĩu aamuonire, akĩmũnyiita, agĩkoma nake na hinya.
English: Shechem the son of Hamor the Hivite, the prince of the land, saw her. He took her, lay with her, and humbled her.

Kikuyu: Na ngoro yake ĩkĩguucĩrĩrio nĩ Dina mwarĩ ũcio wa Jakubu, na akĩenda mũirĩtu ũcio na akĩmwarĩria wega.
English: His soul joined to Dinah, the daughter of Jacob, and he loved the young lady, and spoke kindly to the young lady.

Kikuyu: Nake Shekemu akĩĩra ithe Hamoru atĩrĩ, “Njarĩrĩria harĩ ithe wa mũirĩtu ũyũ atuĩke mũtumia wakwa.”
English: Shechem spoke to his father, Hamor, saying, “Get me this young lady as a wife.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jakubu aiguire atĩ mwarĩ Dina nĩarĩkĩtie gũthũkio-rĩ, na tondũ ariũ ake maarĩ gĩthaka-inĩ na mahiũ make, agĩkira na ũhoro ũcio o nginya rĩrĩa mainũkire.
English: Now Jacob heard that he had defiled Dinah, his daughter; and his sons were with his livestock in the field. Jacob held his peace until they came.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Hamoru ithe wa Shekemu agĩthiĩ kwaria na Jakubu.
English: Hamor the father of Shechem went out to Jacob to talk with him.

Kikuyu: Nao ariũ a Jakubu nĩmainũkĩte kuuma mĩgũnda-inĩ maigua ũrĩa gwekĩkĩte. Nao makĩiyũrwo nĩ kĩeha na mangʼũrĩ, tondũ Shekemu nĩekĩte ũndũ wa thoni thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli, nĩ gũkoma na mwarĩ wa Jakubu, ũndũ ũrĩa ũtagĩrĩirwo nĩ gwĩkwo.
English: The sons of Jacob came in from the field when they heard it. The men were grieved, and they were very angry, because he had done folly in Israel in lying with Jacob’s daughter, a thing that ought not to be done.

Kikuyu: No Hamoru akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ngoro ya mũrũ wakwa Shekemu nĩĩguucĩrĩirio nĩ mwarĩ wanyu. Ndamũthaitha ĩtĩkĩrai amũhikie atuĩke mũtumia wake.
English: Hamor talked with them, saying, “The soul of my son, Shechem, longs for your daughter. Please give her to him as a wife.

Kikuyu: Rekei tũhikanagie; mũtũheage airĩtu anyu na inyuĩ tũkamũheaga airĩtu aitũ.
English: Make marriages with us. Give your daughters to us, and take our daughters for yourselves.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ no mũtũũre hamwe na ithuĩ; bũrũri ũyũ gũtirĩ handũ mũrigĩrĩirio. Tũũrai kuo, mwonjorithagie kuo, na mwĩgĩĩre na indo ciaguo.”
English: You shall dwell with us, and the land will be before you. Live and trade in it, and get possessions in it.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Shekemu akĩĩra ithe wa Dina na ariũ a ithe atĩrĩ, “Rekei njĩtĩkĩrĩke maitho-inĩ manyu na nĩngũmũhe o kĩrĩa gĩothe mũngĩnjĩtia.
English: Shechem said to her father and to her brothers, “Let me find favor in your eyes, and whatever you will tell me I will give.

Kikuyu: Njĩtiai rũracio rwa mũhiki na kĩheo kĩrĩa ingĩrehe, o mũigana ũrĩa mũngĩenda, na nĩngũrĩha o kĩrĩa gĩothe mũngĩnjĩtia. No ndamũthaitha mũũhe mũirĩtu ũyũ atuĩke mũtumia wakwa.”
English: Ask me a great amount for a dowry, and I will give whatever you ask of me, but give me the young lady as a wife.”

Kikuyu: Na tondũ mwarĩ wa nyina Dina nĩarĩkĩtie gũthũkio-rĩ, ariũ a Jakubu rĩrĩa maaragia na Shekemu na ithe Hamoru, magĩcookia ũhoro ũcio na njĩra ya maheeni.
English: The sons of Jacob answered Shechem and Hamor his father with deceit when they spoke, because he had defiled Dinah their sister,

Kikuyu: Makĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Tũtingĩĩka ũndũ ta ũcio; tũtingĩheana mwarĩ witũ kũrĩ mũndũ ũtarĩ mũruu. Ũndũ ũcio no ũtuĩke wa gũtũnyararithia.
English: and said to them, “We can’t do this thing, to give our sister to one who is uncircumcised; for that is a reproach to us.

Kikuyu: Tũkũmwĩtĩkĩria tondũ wa gĩtũmi o kĩmwe tu; inyuĩ mũtuĩke ta ithuĩ na ũndũ wa kũruithia arũme anyu othe.
English: Only on this condition will we consent to you. If you will be as we are, that every male of you be circumcised,

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩmũrĩhikagia airĩtu aitũ na nĩtũrĩhikagia airĩtu anyu. Nĩtũgũtũũrania na inyuĩ na tũtuĩke andũ amwe.
English: then will we give our daughters to you; and we will take your daughters to us, and we will dwell with you, and we will become one people.

Kikuyu: No angĩkorwo mũtigwĩtĩkĩra kũrua-rĩ, tũkuoya mwarĩ witũ twĩthiĩre.”
English: But if you will not listen to us and be circumcised, then we will take our sister,† and we will be gone.”

Kikuyu: Ũhoro ũcio ũkĩoneka ũrĩ mwega nĩ Hamoru na mũriũ Shekemu.
English: Their words pleased Hamor and Shechem, Hamor’s son.

Kikuyu: Tondũ nĩwe warĩ mũtĩĩe mũno kũrĩ andũ othe a nyũmba ya ithe, mwanake ũcio ndaateire ihinda rĩa gwĩka ũrĩa moigire, tondũ nĩakenetio nĩ mwarĩ wa Jakubu.
English: The young man didn’t wait to do this thing, because he had delight in Jacob’s daughter, and he was honored above all the house of his father.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Hamoru na mũriũ Shekemu magĩthiĩ kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra rĩao inene makaarie na andũ ao a itũũra rĩu.*
English: Hamor and Shechem, his son, came to the gate of their city, and talked with the men of their city, saying,

Kikuyu: Nao makiuga atĩrĩ, “Andũ aya nĩ arata aitũ; rekei matũũre bũrũri-inĩ witũ na monjorithagie kuo; bũrũri ũyũ nĩ mũnene wa kũmaigana. Ithuĩ no tũhikie airĩtu ao, nao no mahikie airĩtu aitũ.
English: “These men are peaceful with us. Therefore let them live in the land and trade in it. For behold, the land is large enough for them. Let’s take their daughters to us for wives, and let’s give them our daughters.

Kikuyu: No andũ acio mangĩtĩkĩra gũtũũrania na ithuĩ ta rũrĩrĩ rũmwe angĩkorwo arũme aitũ othe nĩmekũrua, o ta ũrĩa o maruĩte.
English: Only on this condition will the men consent to us to live with us, to become one people, if every male among us is circumcised, as they are circumcised.

Kikuyu: Githĩ mahiũ mao, na indo ciao ciothe, o na nyamũ iria ingĩ ciao ciothe itigũgĩtuĩka ciitũ? Nĩ ũndũ ũcio rekei tũmetĩkĩre, na nĩ megũtũũrania na ithuĩ.”
English: Won’t their livestock and their possessions and all their animals be ours? Only let’s give our consent to them, and they will dwell with us.”

Kikuyu: Arũme arĩa othe maathiĩte kĩhingo-inĩ gĩa itũũra rĩu inene magĩĩtĩkania na Hamoru na mũriũ Shekemu, na arũme othe a itũũra rĩu makĩrua.
English: All who went out of the gate of his city listened to Hamor, and to Shechem his son; and every male was circumcised, all who went out of the gate of his city.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa mĩthenya ĩtatũ, hĩndĩ ĩrĩa othe maarĩ na ruo ariũ eerĩ a Jakubu, Simeoni na Lawi, ariũ a nyina na Dina, makĩoya hiũ ciao cia njora magĩtharĩkĩra itũũra rĩu hĩndĩ ĩrĩa rĩtekũũaga makĩũraga arũme othe.
English: On the third day, when they were sore, two of Jacob’s sons, Simeon and Levi, Dinah’s brothers, each took his sword, came upon the unsuspecting city, and killed all the males.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ makĩũraga Hamoru na mũriũ Shekemu na rũhiũ rwa njora, na makĩruta Dina nyũmba-inĩ ya Shekemu, magĩĩthiĩra.
English: They killed Hamor and Shechem, his son, with the edge of the sword, and took Dinah out of Shechem’s house, and went away.

Kikuyu: Ariũ arĩa angĩ a Jakubu magĩkinya kũu ciimba ciarĩ, na magĩĩtahĩra indo cia itũũra rĩu mwarĩ wa ithe wao aathũkĩirio.
English: Jacob’s sons came on the dead, and plundered the city, because they had defiled their sister.

Kikuyu: Nao magĩtaha ndũũru cia mbũri na cia ngʼombe na ndigiri, na indo iria ingĩ ciao ciothe iria ciarĩ itũũra-inĩ rĩu inene, na iria ciarĩ mĩgũnda-inĩ.
English: They took their flocks, their herds, their donkeys, that which was in the city, that which was in the field,

Kikuyu: Na magĩkuua ũtonga wothe wa itũũra, na atumia othe na ciana, magĩtaha indo ciothe iria ciarĩ nyũmba-inĩ icio.
English: and all their wealth. They took captive all their little ones and their wives, and took as plunder everything that was in the house.

Kikuyu: Nake Jakubu akĩĩra Simeoni na Lawi atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwandehera thĩĩna nĩ ũndũ wa gũtũma menwo nĩ Akaanani na Aperizi, andũ arĩa matũũraga bũrũri ũyũ. Ithuĩ tũrĩ anini tũgĩtarwo, na mangĩnyiitana manjũkĩrĩre na matharĩkĩre-rĩ, niĩ na nyũmba yakwa no tũniinwo.”
English: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, “You have troubled me, to make me odious to the inhabitants of the land, among the Canaanites and the Perizzites. I am few in number. They will gather themselves together against me and strike me, and I will be destroyed, I and my house.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩkũraagĩrĩire atue mwarĩ wa maitũ ta mũmaraya?” 35
English: They said, “Should he deal with our sister as with a prostitute?” 35

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa ũguo Ngai akĩĩra Jakubu atĩrĩ, “Ambata, ũthiĩ Betheli ũgatũũre kuo, na wakĩre Ngai kĩgongona kuo, o we ũrĩa wakuumĩrĩire rĩrĩa woragĩra mũrũ wa nyũkwa Esaũ.”
English: God said to Jacob, “Arise, go up to Bethel, and live there. Make there an altar to God, who appeared to you when you fled from the face of Esau your brother.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jakubu akĩĩra andũ a nyũmba yake, na arĩa othe maarĩ nao, atĩrĩ, “Eheriai ngai cia mĩhianano iria mũrĩ nacio, na mwĩtherie inyuĩ ene, na mwĩkĩre nguo ingĩ.
English: Then Jacob said to his household, and to all who were with him, “Put away the foreign gods that are among you, purify yourselves, and change your garments.

Kikuyu: Mũcooke mũũkĩre, mũreke tũthiĩ twambate Betheli, ngaakĩre Ngai kĩgongona kuo, ũrĩa wanjĩtĩkire mũthenya ũrĩa ndaarĩ thĩĩna-inĩ, na ũrĩa ũkoretwo na niĩ kũrĩa guothe ndanathiĩ.”
English: Let’s arise, and go up to Bethel. I will make there an altar to God, who answered me in the day of my distress, and was with me on the way which I went.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio makĩnengera Jakubu ngai cia mĩhianano iria maarĩ nacio, na irengeeri iria ciarĩ matũ mao,* nake Jakubu agĩcithika gĩtina-inĩ kĩa mũgandi kũu Shekemu.
English: They gave to Jacob all the foreign gods which were in their hands, and the rings which were in their ears; and Jacob hid them under the oak which was by Shechem.

Kikuyu: Nao makiumagara, magĩĩthiĩra, namo matũũra marĩa maamathiũrũrũkĩirie makĩnyiitwo nĩ guoya wa Ngai, nĩ ũndũ ũcio gũtirĩ o na ũmwe wamaingatithirie.
English: They traveled, and a terror of God was on the cities that were around them, and they didn’t pursue the sons of Jacob.

Kikuyu: Jakubu na andũ arĩa othe aarĩ nao magĩkinya Luzu (na nĩkuo Betheli), bũrũri-inĩ wa Kaanani.
English: So Jacob came to Luz (that is, Bethel), which is in the land of Canaan, he and all the people who were with him.

Kikuyu: Agĩaka kĩgongona kũu, na agĩĩta handũ hau Eli-Betheli, tondũ hau nĩho Ngai aamwĩguũrĩirie rĩrĩa ooragĩra mũrũ wa nyina.
English: He built an altar there, and called the place El Beth El; because there God was revealed to him, when he fled from the face of his brother.

Kikuyu: Nake Debora mũreri wa Rebeka agĩkua na agĩthikwo gĩtina-inĩ kĩa mũgandi mũhuro wa Betheli. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio handũ hau hagĩĩtwo Aloni-Bakuthu.
English: Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died, and she was buried below Bethel under the oak; and its name was called Allon Bacuth.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa Jakubu gũcooka kuuma Padani-Aramu, Ngai akĩmuumĩrĩra rĩngĩ na akĩmũrathima.
English: God appeared to Jacob again, when he came from Paddan Aram, and blessed him.

Kikuyu: Ngai akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Rĩĩtwa rĩaku nĩ Jakubu, no ndũgũcooka gwĩtwo Jakubu rĩngĩ; ũrĩĩtagwo Isiraeli.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩmwĩta Isiraeli.
English: God said to him, “Your name is Jacob. Your name shall not be Jacob any more, but your name will be Israel.” He named him Israel.

Kikuyu: Nake Ngai akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Niĩ nĩ niĩ Ngai Mwene-Hinya-Wothe; ciaranai na mũingĩhe. Rũrĩrĩ na gĩkundi kĩa ndũrĩrĩ nĩikoima harĩwe, na athamaki nĩmakoima mwĩrĩ-inĩ waku.
English: God said to him, “I am God Almighty. Be fruitful and multiply. A nation and a company of nations will be from you, and kings will come out of your body.

Kikuyu: Bũrũri ũrĩa niĩ ndaheire Iburahĩmu na Isaaka, o nawe nĩngakũhe guo, na nĩngaheana bũrũri ũyũ kũrĩ njiaro ciaku iria igooka thuutha waku.”
English: The land which I gave to Abraham and Isaac, I will give it to you, and to your offspring after you I will give the land.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Ngai akĩambata akĩmweherera kuuma handũ hau aamwaragĩria.
English: God went up from him in the place where he spoke with him.

Kikuyu: Jakubu akĩhaanda gĩtugĩ kĩa ihiga handũ hau Ngai aarĩtie nake, agĩgĩitĩrĩria ndibei; agĩcooka agĩgĩitĩrĩria maguta.
English: Jacob set up a pillar in the place where he spoke with him, a pillar of stone. He poured out a drink offering on it, and poured oil on it.

Kikuyu: Jakubu agĩĩta handũ hau Ngai aamwarĩirie Betheli.
English: Jacob called the name of the place where God spoke with him “Bethel”.

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio Jakubu na andũ ake makiuma Betheli magĩthiĩ na mbere na rũgendo. Na matigairie hanini makinye Efiratha-rĩ, Rakeli akĩambĩrĩria kũrũmwo na agĩkorwo arĩ na thĩĩna mũingĩ mũno.
English: They traveled from Bethel. There was still some distance to come to Ephrath, and Rachel travailed. She had hard labor.

Kikuyu: Na tondũ aarĩ na thĩĩna mũingĩ mũno wa gũciara, mũmũciarithia akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tiga gwĩtigĩra, tondũ ũrĩ na kahĩĩ kangĩ.”
English: When she was in hard labor, the midwife said to her, “Don’t be afraid, for now you will have another son.”

Kikuyu: Na atanatuĩkana, tondũ nĩ gũkua aakuaga, agĩtua kahĩĩ kau Beni-Oni. No ithe agĩgatua Benjamini.
English: As her soul was departing (for she died), she named him Benoni,† but his father named him Benjamin.‡

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Rakeli agĩkua na agĩthikwo mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra ya gũthiĩ Efiratha (na nokuo Bethilehemu.)
English: Rachel died, and was buried on the way to Ephrath (also called Bethlehem).

Kikuyu: Jakubu akĩhaanda gĩtugĩ igũrũ rĩa mbĩrĩra yake, na nginya ũmũthĩ ũyũ gĩtugĩ kĩu nĩkĩo kĩonanagia mbĩrĩra ya Rakeli.
English: Jacob set up a pillar on her grave. The same is the Pillar of Rachel’s grave to this day.

Kikuyu: Isiraeli agĩthiĩ o na mbere na rũgendo na akĩamba hema yake mwena ũrĩa ũngĩ wa Migidali-Ederi.
English: Israel traveled, and spread his tent beyond the tower of Eder.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Isiraeli aatũũraga bũrũri ũcio-rĩ, Rubeni agĩthiĩ, agĩkoma na Biliha, thuriya ya ithe, nake Isiraeli akĩigua ũhoro ũcio. Jakubu aarĩ na ariũ ikũmi na eerĩ:
English: While Israel lived in that land, Reuben went and lay with Bilhah, his father’s concubine, and Israel heard of it. Now the sons of Jacob were twelve.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Lea maarĩ: Rubeni irigithathi rĩa Jakubu, na Simeoni, na Lawi, na Juda, na Isakaru, na Zebuluni.
English: The sons of Leah: Reuben (Jacob’s firstborn), Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Rakeli maarĩ: Jusufu na Benjamini.
English: The sons of Rachel: Joseph and Benjamin.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Biliha ndungata ya mũirĩtu ya Rakeli maarĩ: Dani na Nafitali.
English: The sons of Bilhah (Rachel’s servant): Dan and Naphtali.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Zilipa ndungata ya mũirĩtu ya Lea maarĩ: Gadi na Asheri. Acio nĩo maarĩ ariũ a Jakubu, arĩa aaciarĩirwo arĩ kũu Padani-Aramu.
English: The sons of Zilpah (Leah’s servant): Gad and Asher. These are the sons of Jacob, who were born to him in Paddan Aram.

Kikuyu: Na Jakubu agĩkinya mũciĩ kũrĩ ithe Isaaka kũu Mamure, hakuhĩ na Kiriathu-Ariba (nĩkuo Hebironi), kũrĩa Iburahĩmu na Isaaka maatũũrĩte.
English: Jacob came to Isaac his father, to Mamre, to Kiriath Arba (which is Hebron), where Abraham and Isaac lived as foreigners.

Kikuyu: Isaaka aatũũrire muoyo mĩaka igana na mĩrongo ĩnana.
English: The days of Isaac were one hundred eighty years.

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩtuĩkana agĩkua na agĩthikwo hamwe na andũ ao arĩa maakuĩte, arĩ mũkũrũ na arĩ na mĩaka mĩingĩ. Nao ariũ ake Esaũ na Jakubu makĩmũthika. 36
English: Isaac gave up the spirit and died, and was gathered to his people, old and full of days. Esau and Jacob, his sons, buried him. 36

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ũyũ nĩguo ũhoro wa Esaũ (na nowe Edomu).
English: Now this is the history of the generations of Esau (that is, Edom).

Kikuyu: Esaũ ahikirie atumia ake kuuma kũrĩ andũ-a-nja a Kaanani: Ada aarĩ mwarĩ wa Eloni ũrĩa Mũhiti, na Oholibama mwarĩ wa Ana ũrĩa warĩ mwarĩ wa Zibeoni ũrĩa Mũhivi,
English: Esau took his wives from the daughters of Canaan: Adah the daughter of Elon, the Hittite; and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, the daughter of Zibeon, the Hivite;

Kikuyu: o na ningĩ Basemathu ũrĩa warĩ mwarĩ wa Ishumaeli na mwarĩ wa nyina na Nebaiothu.
English: and Basemath, Ishmael’s daughter, sister of Nebaioth.

Kikuyu: Ada agĩciarĩra Esaũ Elifazu, na Basemathu agĩciara Reueli,
English: Adah bore to Esau Eliphaz. Basemath bore Reuel.

Kikuyu: nake Oholibama agĩciara Jeushu, na Jalamu na Kora. Acio nĩo maarĩ ariũ a Esaũ, arĩa aaciarĩirwo kũu Kaanani.
English: Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These are the sons of Esau, who were born to him in the land of Canaan.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Esaũ akĩoya atumia ake na ariũ ake na airĩtu ake na andũ othe a nyũmba yake, o na mahiũ make na nyamũ iria ingĩ ciake, na indo ciothe iria aagĩte nacio kũu Kaanani, na agĩthiĩ bũrũri waraihanĩrĩirie na mũrũ wa nyina Jakubu.
English: Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and all the members of his household, with his livestock, all his animals, and all his possessions, which he had gathered in the land of Canaan, and went into a land away from his brother Jacob.

Kikuyu: Indo ciao ciarĩ nyingĩ mũno ũndũ matangĩahotire gũikarania hamwe; bũrũri ũrĩa maatũũraga ndũngĩahotire kũmaigana eerĩ nĩ ũndũ wa mahiũ mao.
English: For their substance was too great for them to dwell together, and the land of their travels couldn’t bear them because of their livestock.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Esaũ (na nowe Edomu) agĩthiĩ gũtũũra bũrũri wa irĩma wa Seiru.
English: Esau lived in the hill country of Seir. Esau is Edom.

Kikuyu: Ũyũ nĩguo ũhoro wa Esaũ ithe wa andũ a Edomu kũu bũrũri-inĩ wa irĩma wa Seiru.
English: This is the history of the generations of Esau the father of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir:

Kikuyu: Maya nĩmo marĩĩtwa ma ariũ a Esaũ: nĩ Elifazu, mũrũ wa Ada mũtumia wa Esaũ, na Reueli, mũrũ wa Basemathu mũtumia wa Esaũ.
English: these are the names of Esau’s sons: Eliphaz, the son of Adah, the wife of Esau; and Reuel, the son of Basemath, the wife of Esau.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Elifazu nĩ aya: nĩ Temani, na Omari, na Zefo, na Gatamu, na Kenazu.
English: The sons of Eliphaz were Teman, Omar, Zepho, and Gatam, and Kenaz.

Kikuyu: Elifazu mũrũ wa Esaũ o nake nĩ aarĩ na thuriya yetagwo Timina, ĩrĩa yamũciarĩire Amaleki. Acio nĩo maarĩ ariũ a mũrũ wa Ada mũtumia wa Esaũ.
English: Timna was concubine to Eliphaz, Esau’s son; and she bore to Eliphaz Amalek. These are the descendants of Adah, Esau’s wife.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Reueli nĩ aya: nĩ Nahathu, na Zera, na Shama, na Miza. Acio nĩo maarĩ ariũ a mũrũ wa Basemathu mũtumia wa Esaũ.
English: These are the sons of Reuel: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These were the descendants of Basemath, Esau’s wife.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Esaũ arĩa aaciarĩirwo nĩ Oholibama mũtumia wake mwarĩ wa Ana ũrĩa warĩ mwarĩ wa Zibeoni: nĩ Jeushu, na Jalamu, na Kora.
English: These were the sons of Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah, the daughter of Zibeon, Esau’s wife: she bore to Esau Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.

Kikuyu: Nao aya nĩo maarĩ anene thĩinĩ wa njiaro cia Esaũ: Ariũ a Elifazu irigithathi rĩa Esaũ: Anene maarĩ Temani, na Omari, na Zefo, na Kenazu,
English: These are the chiefs of the sons of Esau: the sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau: chief Teman, chief Omar, chief Zepho, chief Kenaz,

Kikuyu: na Kora, na Gatamu, na Amaleki. Acio nĩo anene arĩa Elifazu aaciarĩire kũu Edomu; maarĩ a mũrũ wa Ada.
English: chief Korah, chief Gatam, chief Amalek. These are the chiefs who came of Eliphaz in the land of Edom. These are the sons of Adah.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Reueli mũrũ wa Esaũ: Anene maarĩ Nahathu, na Zera, na Shama, na Miza. Aya nĩo anene arĩa Reueli aaciarĩire kũu Edomu; maarĩ a mũrũ wa Basemathu mũtumia wa Esaũ.
English: These are the sons of Reuel, Esau’s son: chief Nahath, chief Zerah, chief Shammah, chief Mizzah. These are the chiefs who came of Reuel in the land of Edom. These are the sons of Basemath, Esau’s wife.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Oholibama mũtumia wa Esaũ: Anene maarĩ Jeushu, na Jalamu, na Kora. Aya nĩo anene arĩa maaciarirwo nĩ Oholibama mũtumia wa Esaũ mwarĩ wa Ana.
English: These are the sons of Oholibamah, Esau’s wife: chief Jeush, chief Jalam, chief Korah. These are the chiefs who came of Oholibamah the daughter of Anah, Esau’s wife.

Kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ ariũ a Esaũ (na nowe Edomu), na acio nĩo maarĩ anene ao.
English: These are the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and these are their chiefs.

Kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ ariũ a Seiru ũrĩa Mũhori arĩa maatũũraga bũrũri ũcio: Lotani, na Shobali, na Zibeoni, na Ana,
English: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,

Kikuyu: na Dishoni, na Ezeri, na Dishani. Ariũ acio a Seiru nĩo maarĩ anene a Ahori kũu Edomu.
English: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These are the chiefs who came of the Horites, the children of Seir in the land of Edom.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Lotani maarĩ: Hori, na Homani. Timina aarĩ mwarĩ wa nyina na Lotani.
English: The children of Lotan were Hori and Heman. Lotan’s sister was Timna.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Shobali maarĩ: Alivani, na Manahathu, na Ebali, na Shefo, na Onamu.
English: These are the children of Shobal: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Zibeoni maarĩ: Aia, na Ana. Ũyũ nĩwe Ana ũrĩa wonire ithima cia maaĩ mahiũ werũ-inĩ rĩrĩa arĩithagia ndigiri cia ithe Zibeoni.
English: These are the children of Zibeon: Aiah and Anah. This is Anah who found the hot springs in the wilderness, as he fed the donkeys of Zibeon his father.

Kikuyu: Ciana cia Ana ciarĩ: Dishoni, na Oholibama mwarĩ wa Ana.
English: These are the children of Anah: Dishon and Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Dishoni maarĩ: Hemudani, na Eshibani, na Itharani, na Cherani.
English: These are the children of Dishon: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Ezeri maarĩ: Bilihani, na Zaavani, na Akani.
English: These are the children of Ezer: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a Dishani maarĩ Uzu, na Arani.
English: These are the children of Dishan: Uz and Aran.

Kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ anene a Ahori: nĩ Lotani, na Shobali, na Zibeoni, na Ana,
English: These are the chiefs who came of the Horites: chief Lotan, chief Shobal, chief Zibeon, chief Anah,

Kikuyu: na Dishoni, na Ezeri, na Dishani. Acio nĩo maarĩ anene a Ahori, kũringana na ikundi ciao kũu bũrũri wa Seiru.
English: chief Dishon, chief Ezer, and chief Dishan. These are the chiefs who came of the Horites, according to their chiefs in the land of Seir.

Kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ athamaki arĩa maathamakaga Edomu o mbere ya Isiraeli gũtanagĩa na mũthamaki o na ũmwe:
English: These are the kings who reigned in the land of Edom, before any king reigned over the children of Israel.

Kikuyu: Bela mũrũ wa Beori nĩwe watuĩkire mũthamaki wa Edomu. Itũũra rĩake inene rĩetagwo Dinihaba.
English: Bela, the son of Beor, reigned in Edom. The name of his city was Dinhabah.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Bela aakuire, Jobabu mũrũ wa Zera wa kuuma Bozira agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
English: Bela died, and Jobab, the son of Zerah of Bozrah, reigned in his place.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jobabu aakuire, Hushamu wa kuuma bũrũri wa Atemani agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
English: Jobab died, and Husham of the land of the Temanites reigned in his place.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Hushamu aakuire, Hadadi mũrũ wa Bedadi, ũrĩa wahootire Midiani kũu bũrũri wa Moabi, agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake. Itũũra rĩake inene rĩetagwo Avithu.
English: Husham died, and Hadad, the son of Bedad, who struck Midian in the field of Moab, reigned in his place. The name of his city was Avith.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Hadadi aakuire, Samala wa kuuma Masereka agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
English: Hadad died, and Samlah of Masrekah reigned in his place.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Samala aakuire, Shauli wa kuuma Rehobothu, kũu gũkuhĩ na Rũũĩ rwa Farati, agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
English: Samlah died, and Shaul of Rehoboth by the river, reigned in his place.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Shauli aakuire, Baali-Hanani mũrũ wa Akibori agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake.
English: Shaul died, and Baal Hanan the son of Achbor reigned in his place.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Baali-Hanani mũrũ wa Akibori aakuire, Hadadi agĩtuĩka mũthamaki ithenya rĩake. Itũũra rĩake inene rĩetagwo Pau, na mũtumia wake eetagwo Mehetabeli mwarĩ wa Matiredi, ũrĩa warĩ mwarĩ wa Mezahabu.
English: Baal Hanan the son of Achbor died, and Hadar reigned in his place. The name of his city was Pau. His wife’s name was Mehetabel, the daughter of Matred, the daughter of Mezahab.

Kikuyu: Aya nĩo maarĩ anene kuuma kũrĩ njiaro cia Esaũ, kũringana na marĩĩtwa mao, na mĩhĩrĩga, na ngʼongo ciao: Timina, na Aliva, na Jethethu,
English: These are the names of the chiefs who came from Esau, according to their families, after their places, and by their names: chief Timna, chief Alvah, chief Jetheth,

Kikuyu: na Oholibama, na Ela, na Pinoni,
English: chief Oholibamah, chief Elah, chief Pinon,

Kikuyu: na Kenazu, na Temani, na Mibizaru,
English: chief Kenaz, chief Teman, chief Mibzar,

Kikuyu: na Magidieli, na Iramu. Acio nĩo maarĩ anene a Edomu, kũringana na kũrĩa maatũũraga bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩa watuĩkĩte wao. Esaũ ũcio nĩwe warĩ ithe wa andũ a Edomu. 37
English: chief Magdiel, and chief Iram. These are the chiefs of Edom, according to their habitations in the land of their possession. This is Esau, the father of the Edomites. 37

Kikuyu: Jakubu nĩatũũrire bũrũri-inĩ ũrĩa ithe aaikarĩte, nĩguo bũrũri wa Kaanani.
English: Jacob lived in the land of his father’s travels, in the land of Canaan.

Kikuyu: Ũyũ nĩguo ũhoro wa Jakubu. Jusufu arĩ mwanake mũnini wa mĩaka ikũmi na mũgwanja aarĩithagia mbũri marĩ na ariũ a ithe, na ariũ a Biliha, na ariũ a Zilipa, atumia a ithe, nake agĩtwarĩra ithe wao ũhoro mũũru ũkoniĩ ariũ a ithe.
English: This is the history of the generations of Jacob. Joseph, being seventeen years old, was feeding the flock with his brothers. He was a boy with the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his father’s wives. Joseph brought an evil report of them to their father.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Isiraeli nĩendeete Jusufu gũkĩra ariũ arĩa angĩ ake othe, tondũ aamũciarire arĩ mũkũrũ; na nĩamũtumithĩirie kanjũ ya goro ngʼemie wega mũno.
English: Now Israel loved Joseph more than all his children, because he was the son of his old age, and he made him a tunic of many colors.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa ariũ a ithe moonire atĩ ithe nĩamwendete kũmakĩra-rĩ, makĩmũmena na matingĩamwarĩirie kiugo o na kĩmwe kĩega.
English: His brothers saw that their father loved him more than all his brothers, and they hated him, and couldn’t speak peaceably to him.

Kikuyu: Ũtukũ ũmwe Jusufu nĩarootire kĩroto, na rĩrĩa eerire ariũ a ithe ũhoro wakĩo, makĩmũmena makĩria.
English: Joseph dreamed a dream, and he told it to his brothers, and they hated him all the more.

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thikĩrĩriai ũhoro wa kĩroto gĩkĩ ndootete:
English: He said to them, “Please hear this dream which I have dreamed:

Kikuyu: Tũrohaga itĩĩa cia ngano tũrĩ mũgũnda-inĩ, na o rĩmwe gĩtĩĩa gĩakwa kĩrehaanda na igũrũ, nacio itĩĩa cianyu irathiũrũrũkĩria gĩakwa, na irakĩinamĩrĩra.”
English: for behold, we were binding sheaves in the field, and behold, my sheaf arose and also stood upright; and behold, your sheaves came around, and bowed down to my sheaf.”

Kikuyu: Ariũ a ithe makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Anga nĩũrenda gũtũthamakĩra? Anga ti-itherũ nĩũgatwatha?” Nao magĩkĩrĩrĩria kũmũmena nĩ ũndũ wa kĩroto kĩu gĩake, na nĩ ũndũ wa ciugo ciake.
English: His brothers asked him, “Will you indeed reign over us? Will you indeed have dominion over us?” They hated him all the more for his dreams and for his words.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jusufu akĩroota kĩroto kĩngĩ, na akĩĩra ariũ a ithe ũhoro wakĩo. Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Thikĩrĩriai, nĩndĩrarootire kĩroto kĩngĩ, na ihinda rĩĩrĩ, ndĩrarootire riũa, na mweri, na njata ikũmi na ĩmwe ikĩnyinamĩrĩra.”
English: He dreamed yet another dream, and told it to his brothers, and said, “Behold, I have dreamed yet another dream: and behold, the sun and the moon and eleven stars bowed down to me.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa eerire ithe o na ariũ a ithe ũhoro ũcio, ithe akĩmũkũma, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩroto kĩa mũthemba ũrĩkũ kĩu ũrotete? Anga nyũkwa, na niĩ, na ariũ a thoguo nĩtũgooka, na tũinamĩrĩre thĩ mbere yaku?”
English: He told it to his father and to his brothers. His father rebuked him, and said to him, “What is this dream that you have dreamed? Will I and your mother and your brothers indeed come to bow ourselves down to the earth before you?”

Kikuyu: Ariũ a ithe nĩmamũiguĩrĩire ũiru, no ithe agĩikara agĩĩciiragia ũhoro wa irooto icio.
English: His brothers envied him, but his father kept this saying in mind.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ariũ a ithe nĩmathiĩte kũrĩithia mbũri cia ithe wao gũkuhĩ na Shekemu,
English: His brothers went to feed their father’s flock in Shechem.

Kikuyu: nake Isiraeli akĩĩra Jusufu atĩrĩ, “O ta ũrĩa ũũĩ, ariũ a thoguo nĩmararĩithia mbũri gũkuhĩ na Shekemu. Ũka, nĩngũgũtũma kũrĩ o.” Nake akĩmũcookeria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũguo noguo.”
English: Israel said to Joseph, “Aren’t your brothers feeding the flock in Shechem? Come, and I will send you to them.” He said to him, “Here I am.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Isiraeli akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Thiĩ ũkarore kana ariũ a thoguo marĩ o ho o wega, o ũndũ ũmwe na mbũri, na ũnjookerie ũhoro.” Nake akĩmũtũma kuuma Gĩtuamba-inĩ kĩa Hebironi. Rĩrĩa Jusufu aakinyire Shekemu,
English: He said to him, “Go now, see whether it is well with your brothers, and well with the flock; and bring me word again.” So he sent him out of the valley of Hebron, and he came to Shechem.

Kikuyu: mũndũ ũmwe akĩmuona akĩũrũũra mĩgũnda-inĩ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ ũracaria?”
English: A certain man found him, and behold, he was wandering in the field. The man asked him, “What are you looking for?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jusufu agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ariũ a baba ndĩracaria. No ũnjĩĩre kũrĩa mararĩithia mbũri ciao?”
English: He said, “I am looking for my brothers. Tell me, please, where they are feeding the flock.”

Kikuyu: Mũndũ ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩmoimĩte gũkũ. Ndĩraiguire makiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Rekei tũthiĩ Dothani.’ ” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jusufu akĩrũmĩrĩra ariũ a ithe, na akĩmakora hakuhĩ na Dothani.
English: The man said, “They have left here, for I heard them say, ‘Let’s go to Dothan.’ ” Joseph went after his brothers, and found them in Dothan.

Kikuyu: No makĩmuona arĩ haraaya, na ataanakinya harĩa maarĩ, magĩciirĩra kũmũũraga.
English: They saw him afar off, and before he came near to them, they conspired against him to kill him.

Kikuyu: Makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Mũroti ũrĩa nĩokĩte!
English: They said to one another, “Behold, this dreamer comes.

Kikuyu: Ũkai; rekei tũmũũrage na tũmũikie irima rĩmwe rĩa maya, na tuuge atĩ nĩ nyamũ njũru ĩmũrĩĩte. Nĩtũkĩone ũrĩa irooto icio ciake ikaahinga.”
English: Come now therefore, and let’s kill him, and cast him into one of the pits, and we will say, ‘An evil animal has devoured him.’ We will see what will become of his dreams.”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa Rubeni aiguire ũguo, akĩgeria kũhonokia Jusufu kuuma moko-inĩ mao. Akiuga atĩrĩ, “Rekei tũtige kũmũũraga.
English: Reuben heard it, and delivered him out of their hand, and said, “Let’s not take his life.”

Kikuyu: Mũtigaite thakame. Mũikiei irima-inĩ rĩĩrĩ rĩ gũkũ werũ-inĩ, no mũtikamwĩke ũũru.” Rubeni oigaga ũguo nĩgeetha amũhonokie kuuma kũrĩ o, nĩguo amũcookie kũrĩ ithe.
English: Reuben said to them, “Shed no blood. Throw him into this pit that is in the wilderness, but lay no hand on him”—that he might deliver him out of their hand, to restore him to his father.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Jusufu aakinyire harĩ ariũ a ithe, makĩmũruta kanjũ yake ĩrĩa eekĩrĩte, o ĩrĩa yarĩ ya goro na ngʼemie wega mũno.
English: When Joseph came to his brothers, they stripped Joseph of his tunic, the tunic of many colors that was on him;

Kikuyu: Makĩmũnyiita, makĩmũikia irima rĩu. Na rĩrĩ, irima rĩu rĩarĩ rĩũmũ; rĩtiarĩ na maaĩ.
English: and they took him, and threw him into the pit. The pit was empty. There was no water in it.

Kikuyu: Magĩcooka magĩikara thĩ kũrĩa irio ciao, magĩtiira maitho makĩona gĩkundi kĩnene kĩa Aishu- maeli gĩgĩũka kiumĩte Gileadi. Ngamĩĩra ciao ciakuuithĩtio mahuti manungi wega, na ũbani, na ũũkĩ- wa-ngoma ũrĩa wĩtagwo manemane, na maikũrũkĩte mathiĩ matware indo icio Misiri.
English: They sat down to eat bread, and they lifted up their eyes and looked, and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with their camels bearing spices and balm and myrrh, going to carry it down to Egypt.

Kikuyu: Juda akĩĩra ariũ a ithe atĩrĩ, “Nĩ uumithio ũrĩkũ tũkuona tũngĩũraga mũrũ wa ithe witũ na tũhithe gĩkuũ gĩake?
English: Judah said to his brothers, “What profit is it if we kill our brother and conceal his blood?

Kikuyu: Rekei tũmwenderie Aishumaeli aya, no tũtikamwĩke ũũru na moko maitũ, tondũ ũyũ nĩ mũrũ wa ithe witũ, tũrĩ a mũthiimo ũmwe na thakame o ĩmwe.” Nao ariũ a ithe magĩtĩkĩra.
English: Come, and let’s sell him to the Ishmaelites, and not let our hand be on him; for he is our brother, our flesh.” His brothers listened to him.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio hĩndĩ ĩrĩa onjorithia acio Amidiani maahĩtũkagĩra hau, ariũ a ithe makĩruta Jusufu irima, na makĩmwendia cekeri mĩrongo ĩĩrĩ cia betha* kũrĩ Aishumaeli acio, nao makĩmũtwara Misiri.
English: Midianites who were merchants passed by, and they drew and lifted up Joseph out of the pit, and sold Joseph to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver. The merchants brought Joseph into Egypt.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Rubeni aacookire irima-inĩ na akĩona atĩ Jusufu ndaarĩ ho, agĩtembũranga nguo ciake.
English: Reuben returned to the pit, and saw that Joseph wasn’t in the pit; and he tore his clothes.

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka kũrĩ ariũ a ithe akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Kamwana karĩa gatirĩ ho! Niĩ ngwĩka atĩa?”
English: He returned to his brothers, and said, “The child is no more; and I, where will I go?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩoya kanjũ ya Jusufu, magĩthĩnja mbũri na magĩtobokia kanjũ ĩyo thĩinĩ wa thakame ĩyo.
English: They took Joseph’s tunic, and killed a male goat, and dipped the tunic in the blood.

Kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩoya kanjũ ĩyo yagemetio, makĩmĩtwarĩra ithe wao makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Kanjũ ĩno nĩ kũmĩona tũramĩonire. Mĩrore wega wone kana hihi nĩ kanjũ ĩrĩa ya mũrũguo.”
English: They took the tunic of many colors, and they brought it to their father, and said, “We have found this. Examine it, now, and see if it is your son’s tunic or not.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmĩmenya, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kanjũ ya mũrũ wakwa! Nyamũ njũru nĩĩmũrĩĩte. Ti-itherũ Jusufu nĩarĩkĩtie kũũragwo, agatambuurwo icunjĩ.”
English: He recognized it, and said, “It is my son’s tunic. An evil animal has devoured him. Joseph is without doubt torn in pieces.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jakubu agĩtembũranga nguo ciake, agĩĩkĩra nguo ya ikũnia, na agĩcakaĩra mũriũ matukũ maingĩ.
English: Jacob tore his clothes, and put sackcloth on his waist, and mourned for his son many days.

Kikuyu: Ariũ ake othe na airĩtu ake magĩũka kũmũhooreria, no akĩrega kũhoorerio, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aca! Ngathiĩ mbĩrĩra kũrĩ mũrũ wakwa ngĩrĩraga.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ithe agĩthiĩ na mbere kũmũrĩrĩra.
English: All his sons and all his daughters rose up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. He said, “For I will go down to Sheol† to my son, mourning.” His father wept for him.

Kikuyu: Nao Amidiani acio makĩendia† Jusufu kũu Misiri kũrĩ Potifaru, ũmwe wa anene a Firaũni, nake aarĩ mũrũgamĩrĩri wa arangĩri a Firaũni. 38
English: The Midianites sold him into Egypt to Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh’s, the captain of the guard. 38

Kikuyu: Ihinda-inĩ rĩu-rĩ, Juda agĩtiga ariũ a ithe agĩikũrũka agĩthiĩ gũikara na mũndũ wa Adulamu wetagwo Hira.
English: At that time, Judah went down from his brothers, and visited a certain Adullamite, whose name was Hirah.

Kikuyu: Juda arĩ kũu agĩcemania na mwarĩ wa Mũkaanani wetagwo Shua. Akĩmũhikia na agĩkoma nake;
English: There, Judah saw the daughter of a certain Canaanite man named Shua. He took her, and went in to her.

Kikuyu: nake akĩgĩa nda na agĩciara kahĩĩ, gagĩtuuo Eri.
English: She conceived, and bore a son; and he named him Er.

Kikuyu: Akĩgĩa nda ĩngĩ, agĩciara kahĩĩ kangĩ gagĩtuuo Onani.
English: She conceived again, and bore a son; and she named him Onan.

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ agĩciara kahĩĩ kangĩ, agĩgatua Shela. Aagaciarĩire kũu Chezibu.
English: She yet again bore a son, and named him Shelah. He was at Chezib when she bore him.

Kikuyu: Maagimara Juda akĩgũrĩra Eri ũrĩa warĩ irigithathi rĩake, mũtumia wetagwo Tamaru.
English: Judah took a wife for Er, his firstborn, and her name was Tamar.

Kikuyu: No Eri, irigithathi rĩa Juda, aarĩ mwaganu maitho-inĩ ma Jehova; nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jehova akĩmũũraga.
English: Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in Yahweh’s sight. So Yahweh killed him.

Kikuyu: Nake Juda akĩĩra Onani atĩrĩ, “Koma na mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyũkwa, ũmũhingĩrie ũrĩa wagĩrĩirwo nĩkũmwĩka ũrĩ mũrũ wa nyina na mũthuuri wake, nĩguo ũciarĩre mũrũ wa nyũkwa rũciaro.”
English: Judah said to Onan, “Go in to your brother’s wife, and perform the duty of a husband’s brother to her, and raise up offspring for your brother.”

Kikuyu: Nake Onani nĩamenyaga atĩ rũciaro rũu rũtigatuĩka rwake; nĩ ũndũ ũcio o akoma na mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyina, agaita mbeũ ya ũciari thĩ, nĩguo ndagaciarĩre mũrũ wa nyina rũciaro.
English: Onan knew that the offspring wouldn’t be his; and when he went in to his brother’s wife, he spilled his semen on the ground, lest he should give offspring to his brother.

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũcio eekire warĩ mũũru maitho-inĩ ma Jehova; nĩ ũndũ ũcio o nake akĩũragwo nĩ Jehova.
English: The thing which he did was evil in Yahweh’s sight, and he killed him also.

Kikuyu: JudaakĩĩraTamarumũtumiawamũriũatĩrĩ, “Thiĩũtũũreũrĩwandigwagwathoguomũciĩnginya mũrũ wakwa Shela agimare.” Tondũ eeciiririe atĩrĩ, “O nake Shela ahota gũkua ta ariũ a nyina.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Tamaru agĩthiĩ gũtũũra mũciĩ gwa ithe.
English: Then Judah said to Tamar, his daughter-in-law, “Remain a widow in your father’s house, until Shelah, my son, is grown up;” for he said, “Lest he also die, like his brothers.” Tamar went and lived in her father’s house.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa ihinda iraaya, mũtumia wa Juda, mwarĩ wa Shua, agĩkua. Na kahinda ka macakaya gaathira na Juda athirwo nĩ kĩeha, akĩambata agĩthiĩ Timina kũrĩ andũ arĩa meenjaga ngʼondu ciake guoya, na agĩthiĩ hamwe na mũrata wake, Hira wa Adulamu.
English: After many days, Shua’s daughter, the wife of Judah, died. Judah was comforted, and went up to his sheep shearers to Timnah, he and his friend Hirah, the Adullamite.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Tamaru eerirwo atĩrĩ, “Ithe wa mũthuuriguo erekeire Timina kwenja ngʼondu ciake guoya,”
English: Tamar was told, “Behold, your father-in-law is going up to Timnah to shear his sheep.”

Kikuyu: akĩruta nguo ciake cia mũtumia wa ndigwa, akĩĩhumbĩra na taama nginya ũthiũ nĩguo ndakae kũmenyeka, agĩikara thĩ itoonyero-inĩ rĩa Enaimu, rĩrĩa rĩarĩ njĩra-inĩ ya gũthiĩ Timina. Nĩgũkorwo nĩonire atĩ, o na gũtuĩka Shela aarĩ mũndũ mũgima, Juda ndaheanĩte Tamaru kũrĩ we atuĩke mũtumia wake.
English: She took off the garments of her widowhood, and covered herself with her veil, and wrapped herself, and sat in the gate of Enaim, which is on the way to Timnah; for she saw that Shelah was grown up, and she wasn’t given to him as a wife.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Juda aamuonire-rĩ, agĩĩciiria atĩ Tamaru aarĩ mũmaraya* tondũ nĩehumbĩrĩte ũthiũ.
English: When Judah saw her, he thought that she was a prostitute, for she had covered her face.

Kikuyu: Na tondũ ndooĩ atĩ aarĩ mũtumia wa mũriũ, agĩthiĩ harĩ we mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka, ĩtĩkĩra ngome nawe.” Mũtumia ũcio akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Na nĩ kĩĩ ũkũũhe nĩguo ngome nawe?”
English: He turned to her by the way, and said, “Please come, let me come in to you,” for he didn’t know that she was his daughter-in-law. She said, “What will you give me, that you may come in to me?”

Kikuyu: Juda akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩngũgũtũmĩra koori kuuma rũũru-inĩ rwakwa.” Mũtumia ũcio akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩũkũndigĩra kĩndũ gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra kĩĩranĩro gĩaku nginya rĩrĩa ũgaakaneana?”
English: He said, “I will send you a young goat from the flock.” She said, “Will you give me a pledge, until you send it?”

Kikuyu: Juda akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũkwenda ngũtigĩre kĩĩ gĩa kũrũgamĩrĩra?” Mũtumia ũcio akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ndigĩra gĩcũhĩ kĩa rũũri rwaku na rũrigi rwakĩo, o na rũthanju rũu rũrĩ guoko-inĩ gwaku.” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Juda agĩtigĩra mũtumia ũcio indo icio na agĩkoma nake; na Tamaru akĩgĩa nda yake.
English: He said, “What pledge will I give you?” She said, “Your signet and your cord, and your staff that is in your hand.” He gave them to her, and came in to her, and she conceived by him.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia ũcio aarĩkia gwĩthiĩra, akĩaũra taama wake, akĩĩhumba nguo ciake cia mũtumia wa ndigwa rĩngĩ.
English: She arose, and went away, and put off her veil from her, and put on the garments of her widowhood.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Juda atũmire mũratawe ũcio wa kũu Adulamu na koori nĩgeetha acookerio indo icio cia kũrũgamĩrĩra kuuma kũrĩ mũtumia ũcio-rĩ, ndaamuonire.
English: Judah sent the young goat by the hand of his friend, the Adullamite, to receive the pledge from the woman’s hand, but he didn’t find her.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩũria andũ arĩa maatũũraga kũu atĩrĩ, “Arĩ ha mũmaraya ũrĩa ũraarĩ haha mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra ya gũthiĩ Enaimu?” Nao magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Haha hatirĩ hakorwo na mũmaraya.”
English: Then he asked the men of her place, saying, “Where is the prostitute, that was at Enaim by the road?” They said, “There has been no prostitute here.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩcooka kũrĩ Juda, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndinamuona. Hamwe na ũguo-rĩ, andũ arĩa matũũraga hau moigire atĩrĩ, ‘Haha hatirĩ hakorwo na mũmaraya.’ ”
English: He returned to Judah, and said, “I haven’t found her; and also the men of the place said, ‘There has been no prostitute here.’ ”

Kikuyu: NakeJudaakiugaatĩrĩ, “Nĩakĩrekwoaikarenakĩrĩaarĩnakĩo, kanatũtuĩkeagũthekererwo. Ningĩ- rĩ, nĩngũmũtũmĩire koori gaka, no ndũnamuona.”
English: Judah said, “Let her keep it, lest we be shamed. Behold, I sent this young goat, and you haven’t found her.”

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa mĩeri ĩtatũ, Juda akĩĩrwo atĩrĩ, “Tamaru mũtumia wa mũrũguo nĩehĩtie, akahũũra ũmaraya, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio arĩ na nda.” Juda akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mumiei mũmũrehe, acinwo akue!”
English: About three months later, Judah was told, “Tamar, your daughter-in-law, has played the prostitute. Moreover, behold, she is with child by prostitution.” Judah said, “Bring her out, and let her be burned.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa oimagio-rĩ, agĩtũma ndũmĩrĩri kũrĩ ithe wa mũthuuriwe, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩ na nda ya mũndũ ũrĩa mwene indo ici.” Agĩcooka akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ta rora kana no ũmenye nũũ mwene gĩcũhĩ gĩkĩ kĩ na mũhũũri, na rũrigi rũrũ, o na rũthanju rũrũ.”
English: When she was brought out, she sent to her father-in-law, saying, “I am with child by the man who owns these.” She also said, “Please discern whose these are—the signet, and the cords, and the staff.”

Kikuyu: Juda agĩcimenya, na akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũtumia ũyũ nĩ mũthingu kũngĩra; ekĩte ũguo nĩgũkorwo ndiamũheanire kũrĩ mũrũ wakwa Shela.” Juda ndaacookire gũkoma nake rĩngĩ.
English: Judah acknowledged them, and said, “She is more righteous than I, because I didn’t give her to Shelah, my son.” He knew her again no more.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa ihinda rĩake rĩakinyire rĩa gũciara-rĩ, kwarĩ na tũhĩĩ twĩrĩ twa mahatha nda yake.
English: In the time of her travail, behold, twins were in her womb.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aaciaraga-rĩ, kamwe gatuo gagĩcomora guoko; nake mũmũciarithia akĩoya uthi mũtune, akĩwoherera guoko-inĩ gwako, na akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe woima mbere.”
English: When she travailed, one put out a hand, and the midwife took and tied a scarlet thread on his hand, saying, “This came out first.”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa aacookirie guoko na thĩinĩ, mũrũ wa nyina akiuma, nake mũciarithania akiuga atĩrĩ, “Githĩ nĩguo weturĩra njĩra!” Nake agĩtuuo Perezu.†
English: As he drew back his hand, behold, his brother came out, and she said, “Why have you made a breach for yourself?” Therefore his name was called Perez.†

Kikuyu: Nake mũrũ wa nyina ũrĩa warĩ na uthi mũtune guoko-inĩ gwake akiuma, nake agĩtuuo Zera. 39
English: Afterward his brother came out, who had the scarlet thread on his hand, and his name was called Zerah.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jusufu nĩaikũrũkirio o nginya bũrũri wa Misiri. Nake Potifaru, Mũmisiri ũmwe wa anene a Firaũni, na mũrũgamĩrĩri wa arangĩri a Firaũni, akĩmũgũra kuuma kũrĩ Aishumaeli arĩa maamũtwarĩte kũu.
English: Joseph was brought down to Egypt. Potiphar, an officer of Pharaoh’s, the captain of the guard, an Egyptian, bought him from the hand of the Ishmaelites that had brought him down there.

Kikuyu: Nake Jehova aarĩ hamwe na Jusufu, nake akĩgaacĩra, na agĩtũũra nyũmba-inĩ ya Mũmisiri ũcio mwathi wake.
English: Yahweh was with Joseph, and he was a prosperous man. He was in the house of his master the Egyptian.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mwathi wake onire atĩ Jehova aarĩ hamwe nake, na atĩ Jehova nĩatũmĩte Jusufu agaacĩre maũndũ-inĩ mothe marĩa ekaga-rĩ,
English: His master saw that Yahweh was with him, and that Yahweh made all that he did prosper in his hand.

Kikuyu: Jusufu agĩĩtĩkĩrĩka maitho-inĩ make na agĩtuĩka wa kũmũtungatĩra. Potifaru akĩmũtua mũrori wa nyũmba yake na akĩmwĩhokera ũmenyereri wa kĩrĩa gĩothe aarĩ nakĩo.
English: Joseph found favor in his sight. He ministered to him, and Potiphar made him overseer over his house, and all that he had he put into his hand.

Kikuyu: Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aamũtuire mũrori wa nyũmba yake na kĩrĩa gĩothe aarĩ nakĩo, Jehova akĩrathima nyũmba ya Mũmisiri ũcio nĩ ũndũ wa Jusufu. Kĩrathimo kĩa Jehova kĩarĩ igũrũ wa indo ciothe cia Potifaru, iria ciarĩ nyũmba o na iria ciarĩ mũgũnda.
English: From the time that he made him overseer in his house, and over all that he had, Yahweh blessed the Egyptian’s house for Joseph’s sake. Yahweh’s blessing was on all that he had, in the house and in the field.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtigĩra Jusufu indo ciothe iria aarĩ nacio acimenyagĩrĩre; rĩrĩa rĩothe Jusufu aarĩ mũrũgamĩrĩri-rĩ, Potifaru ndarũmbũyanagia na kĩndũ o nakĩ tiga o irio iria aarĩĩaga.* Na rĩrĩ, Jusufu aarĩ mũthaka na wa mwĩrĩ mwega,
English: He left all that he had in Joseph’s hand. He didn’t concern himself with anything, except for the food which he ate. Joseph was well-built and handsome.

Kikuyu: na thuutha wa ihinda, mũtumia wa mwathi wake akĩmwĩrirĩria, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka ũkome na niĩ!”
English: After these things, his master’s wife set her eyes on Joseph; and she said, “Lie with me.”

Kikuyu: No Jusufu akĩrega. Akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Niĩ ndĩ mũrori-rĩ, mwathi wakwa ndarũmbũyanagia na kĩndũ o nakĩ kĩa nyũmba, indo iria ciothe arĩ nacio nĩanjĩĩhokeire ndĩcimenyagĩrĩre.
English: But he refused, and said to his master’s wife, “Behold, my master doesn’t know what is with me in the house, and he has put all that he has into my hand.

Kikuyu: Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũngĩ mũnene nyũmba ĩno ũngĩrĩte. Mwathi wakwa ndangirĩrĩirie kĩndũ o nakĩ, tiga wee wiki, tondũ ũrĩ mũtumia wake. Ndaakĩhota atĩa gwĩka ũndũ ta ũyũ wa waganu njĩhĩrie Ngai?”
English: No one is greater in this house than I am, and he has not kept back anything from me but you, because you are his wife. How then can I do this great wickedness, and sin against God?”

Kikuyu: Na o na gũtuĩka nĩaringagĩrĩria Jusufu mũthenya o mũthenya akome nake-rĩ, Jusufu nĩaregire gũkoma nake o na kana gũkorwo hamwe nake.
English: As she spoke to Joseph day by day, he didn’t listen to her, to lie by her, or to be with her.

Kikuyu: No mũthenya ũmwe Jusufu nĩaingĩrire nyũmba kũruta wĩra wake, na gũtiarĩ ndungata o na ĩmwe ya nyũmba yarĩ kũu thĩinĩ.
English: About this time, he went into the house to do his work, and there were none of the men of the house inside.

Kikuyu: Mũtumia ũcio akĩnyiita nguo ya Jusufu, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka ũkome na niĩ!” No Jusufu agĩtiga nguo yake guoko-inĩ kwa mũtumia ũcio, akiuma nyũmba akĩũra.
English: She caught him by his garment, saying, “Lie with me!” He left his garment in her hand, and ran outside.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mũtumia ũcio onire atĩ Jusufu nĩatiga nguo yake guoko-inĩ gwake na oora-rĩ,
English: When she saw that he had left his garment in her hand, and had run outside,

Kikuyu: agĩĩta ndungata cia nyũmba yake, agĩciĩra atĩrĩ, “Ta kĩonei Mũhibirania ũyũ aarehirwo kũrĩ ithuĩ nĩgeetha atũnyarare! Egũũkĩte haha nĩguo akome na niĩ, na niĩ ndakaya.
English: she called to the men of her house, and spoke to them, saying, “Behold, he has brought a Hebrew in to us to mock us. He came in to me to lie with me, and I cried with a loud voice.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aigua ngĩkaya ndeithio, aatiga nguo yake haha, oima nyũmba na oora.”
English: When he heard that I lifted up my voice and cried, he left his garment by me, and ran outside.”

Kikuyu: Nake agĩikara na nguo ĩyo nginya rĩrĩa mwathi wa Jusufu aainũkire.
English: She laid up his garment by her, until his master came home.

Kikuyu: Nake mũtumia ũcio akĩmũhe rũgano rũu, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ngombo ĩrĩa ya Mũhibirania, ĩrĩa watũreheire ĩgũũkĩte kũrĩ niĩ kũnyarara.
English: She spoke to him according to these words, saying, “The Hebrew servant, whom you have brought to us, came in to me to mock me,

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa njugire mbu ndeithio, yatiga nguo ĩno harĩ niĩ, yoima nyũmba na yora.”
English: and as I lifted up my voice and cried, he left his garment by me, and ran outside.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa mwathi wa Jusufu aiguire rũgano rũu aaheirwo nĩ mũtumia wake, rĩrĩa aamwĩrire, “Ũguo nĩguo ngombo yaku ĩnjĩkire,” agĩcinwo nĩ marakara.
English: When his master heard the words of his wife, which she spoke to him, saying, “This is what your servant did to me,” his wrath was kindled.

Kikuyu: Nake mwathi wa Jusufu akĩnyiita Jusufu akĩmũikia njeera, kũndũ kũrĩa gwaikagio andũ arĩa oohe a mũthamaki. No hĩndĩ ĩyo Jusufu aarĩ kũu njeera-rĩ,
English: Joseph’s master took him, and put him into the prison, the place where the king’s prisoners were bound, and he was there in custody.

Kikuyu: Jehova aarĩ hamwe nake; akĩmũtuga na agĩtũma etĩkĩrĩke maitho-inĩ ma mũnene wa njeera.
English: But Yahweh was with Joseph, and showed kindness to him, and gave him favor in the sight of the keeper of the prison.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũnene wa njeera agĩtua Jusufu mũrori wa arĩa othe moohetwo njeera, na agĩtuuo mũmenyereri wa maũndũ mothe marĩa meekagwo kũu.
English: The keeper of the prison committed to Joseph’s hand all the prisoners who were in the prison. Whatever they did there, he was responsible for it.

Kikuyu: Mũnene ũcio wa njeera ndaarũmbũyagia ũndũ o na ũmwe wa marĩa maamenyagĩrĩrwo nĩ Jusufu, tondũ Jehova aarĩ hamwe na Jusufu na akĩmũhe ũgaacĩru maũndũ-inĩ mothe marĩa ekaga. 40
English: The keeper of the prison didn’t look after anything that was under his hand, because Yahweh was with him; and that which he did, Yahweh made it prosper. 40

Kikuyu: Thuutha ũcio-rĩ, mũndũ ũrĩa watwaragĩra mũthamaki ndibei, na mũndũ ũrĩa wathondekagĩra mũthamaki wa Misiri mĩgate-rĩ, makĩhĩtĩria mwathi wao, ũcio mũthamaki wa Misiri.
English: After these things, the butler of the king of Egypt and his baker offended their lord, the king of Egypt.

Kikuyu: Firaũni mũthamaki ũcio akĩrakario nĩ anene acio ake eerĩ, mũnene wa arĩa maamũtwaragĩra ndibei na mũnene wa arĩa maamũthondekagĩra mĩgate,
English: Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief cup bearer and the chief baker.

Kikuyu: na akĩmahingĩrithia nyũmba ya mũnene wa arĩa arangĩri njeera, o ĩrĩa Jusufu oheetwo.
English: He put them in custody in the house of the captain of the guard, into the prison, the place where Joseph was bound.

Kikuyu: Mũnene wa arangĩri akĩmaneana kũrĩ Jusufu amarorage, nake Jusufu akĩmatungatĩra. Na thuutha wa gũikara kĩoho-inĩ kwa ihinda-rĩ,
English: The captain of the guard assigned them to Joseph, and he took care of them. They stayed in prison many days.

Kikuyu: o ũmwe wa acio eerĩ, ũrĩa watwaragĩra mũthamaki ndibei na mũthondeki wa mĩgate ya mũthamaki wa Misiri, o acio maahingĩirwo njeera, makĩroota irooto ũtukũ ũmwe, na o kĩroto kĩarĩ na mũtaũrĩre wakĩo.
English: They both dreamed a dream, each man his dream, in one night, each man according to the interpretation of his dream, the cup bearer and the baker of the king of Egypt, who were bound in the prison.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jusufu aathiire kũrĩ o rũciinĩ rwa mũthenya ũyũ ũngĩ-rĩ, akĩona marĩ na kĩeha.
English: Joseph came in to them in the morning, and saw them, and saw that they were sad.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩũria anene acio a Firaũni, o acio moohetwo hamwe nake nyũmba-inĩ ya mwathi wake, atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmĩte mũtukie ithiithi cianyu ũmũthĩ?”
English: He asked Pharaoh’s officers who were with him in custody in his master’s house, saying, “Why do you look so sad today?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ithuĩ ithuerĩ nĩtũrotete irooto, na gũtirĩ mũndũ wa gũcitaũra.” Jusufu akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Githĩ ũtaũri ti wa Ngai? Njĩĩrai irooto cianyu.”
English: They said to him, “We have dreamed a dream, and there is no one who can interpret it.” Joseph said to them, “Don’t interpretations belong to God? Please tell it to me.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũnene wa arĩa maatwaragĩra mũthamaki ndibei akĩĩra Jusufu kĩroto gĩake. Akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Kĩroto-inĩ gĩakwa ndĩronire mũthabibũ ũrĩ mbere yakwa,
English: The chief cup bearer told his dream to Joseph, and said to him, “In my dream, behold, a vine was in front of me,

Kikuyu: na ũraarĩ na honge ithatũ. Nacio iraaruta kĩro o ũguo iracanũka, nacio imanjĩka cia thabibũ irerua.
English: and in the vine were three branches. It was as though it budded, it blossomed, and its clusters produced ripe grapes.

Kikuyu: Na niĩ ndĩrakorwo nyiitĩte gĩkombe kĩa Firaũni na guoko, ndĩrooya thabibũ icio, ndĩraacihihĩra gĩkombe-inĩ kĩa Firaũni, na ndĩramũnengera.”
English: Pharaoh’s cup was in my hand; and I took the grapes, and pressed them into Pharaoh’s cup, and I gave the cup into Pharaoh’s hand.”

Kikuyu: Jusufu akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũtaũri wakĩo nĩ ũyũ: Honge icio ithatũ nĩ mĩthenya ĩtatũ.
English: Joseph said to him, “This is its interpretation: the three branches are three days.

Kikuyu: Mĩthenya ĩtatũ ĩtanathira-rĩ, Firaũni nĩegũgũtũũgĩria na agũcookie wĩra-inĩ waku, na ũcooke kũnengagĩra Firaũni gĩkombe gĩake, o ta ũrĩa wekaga rĩrĩa warĩ mũmũhei ndibei.
English: Within three more days, Pharaoh will lift up your head, and restore you to your office. You will give Pharaoh’s cup into his hand, the way you did when you were his cup bearer.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa maũndũ maku makaagaacĩra-rĩ, ũkandirikana ũnjiguĩre tha, ũngweete harĩ Firaũni, ũndute gũkũ njeera.
English: But remember me when it is well with you. Please show kindness to me, and make mention of me to Pharaoh, and bring me out of this house.

Kikuyu: Tondũ nĩkũnyiitwo ndaanyiitirwo na hinya, ngĩrutwo bũrũri wa Ahibirania, na ningĩ o na gũkũ gũtirĩ ũndũ mũũru ndeekĩte wa gũtũma njikio njeera.”
English: For indeed, I was stolen away out of the land of the Hebrews, and here also I have done nothing that they should put me into the dungeon.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa mũnene wa arĩa athondeki mĩgate onire atĩ Jusufu nĩataũra kĩroto kĩu wega-rĩ, akĩĩra Jusufu atĩrĩ, “O na niĩ nĩndĩrarootete: Ndĩrakuuĩte ikabũ ithatũ cia mĩgate na mũtwe.
English: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was good, he said to Joseph, “I also was in my dream, and behold, three baskets of white bread were on my head.

Kikuyu: Gĩkabũ kĩrĩa kĩrarigĩtie igũrũ kĩraarĩ na mĩgate ya mĩthemba yothe ĩthondekeirwo Firaũni; no nacio nyoni iramĩrĩĩaga ĩrĩ o gĩkabũ-inĩ ndĩgĩkuuĩte na mũtwe.”
English: In the uppermost basket there were all kinds of baked food for Pharaoh, and the birds ate them out of the basket on my head.”

Kikuyu: Jusufu akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũtaũri wakĩo nĩ ũyũ: Ikabũ icio ithatũ nĩ mĩthenya ĩtatũ.
