Kikuyu: Akĩmarathima mũthenya ũcio, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Isiraeli akaarathimanaga akĩgwetaga rĩĩtwa rĩaku akoiga atĩrĩ: ‘Ngai arotũma ũtuĩke o ta Efiraimu na Manase.’ ” Nĩ ũndũ ũcio akĩgweta Efiraimu mbere ya Manase.
English: He blessed them that day, saying, “Israel will bless in your name, saying, ‘God make you as Ephraim and as Manasseh’ ” He set Ephraim before Manasseh.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Isiraeli akĩĩra Jusufu atĩrĩ, “Ndĩ hakuhĩ gũkua, no Ngai nĩegũkorwo hamwe na inyuĩ, na nĩakamũcookia bũrũri-inĩ wa maithe manyu.
English: Israel said to Joseph, “Behold, I am dying, but God will be with you, and bring you again to the land of your fathers.

Kikuyu: Nawe, ũrĩ ta mwathi wa ariũ a thoguo-rĩ, nĩndakũhe rũgongo rwa bũrũri ũrĩa ndaatunyire Aamori na rũhiũ rwakwa rwa njora na ũta wakwa.” 49
English: Moreover I have given to you one portion above your brothers, which I took out of the hand of the Amorite with my sword and with my bow.” 49

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jakubu agĩtũmanĩra ariũ ake, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Cookanĩrĩrai haha nĩgeetha ndĩmwĩre ũrĩa gũgekĩka kũrĩ inyuĩ matukũ-inĩ marĩa magooka.
English: Jacob called to his sons, and said: “Gather yourselves together, that I may tell you that which will happen to you in the days to come.

Kikuyu: “Ũnganai na mũthikĩrĩrie, inyuĩ ariũ a Jakubu; thikĩrĩriai Isiraeli ithe wanyu.
English: Assemble yourselves, and hear, you sons of Jacob. Listen to Israel, your father.

Kikuyu: “Rubeni, ũrĩ irigithathi rĩakwa, ũhoti wakwa, kĩonithania kĩa mbere kĩa hinya wakwa; ũkĩrĩte arĩa angĩ na gũtĩĩka, ũkamakĩra na hinya.
English: “Reuben, you are my firstborn, my might, and the beginning of my strength, excelling in dignity, and excelling in power.

Kikuyu: Wagagĩte ta maaĩ, na nĩ ũndũ ũcio ndũgũcooka gũkĩra arĩa angĩ, tondũ nĩwahaicire ũrĩrĩ wa thoguo, ũgĩthaahia ũrĩrĩ wakwa.
English: Boiling over like water, you shall not excel, because you went up to your father’s bed, then defiled it. He went up to my couch.

Kikuyu: “Simeoni na Lawi nĩ a nyina ũmwe, hiũ ciao cia njora nĩ indo cia mbaara cia ũhinya.
English: “Simeon and Levi are brothers. Their swords are weapons of violence.

Kikuyu: Ngoro yakwa ndũkanatoonye ndundu-inĩ yao, ndũkananyiitane na kĩũngano kĩao, nĩgũkorwo nĩmooragĩte andũ marakarĩte, na magatemanga ndegwa magũrũ macithuithie o ũrĩa mangĩendire.
English: My soul, don’t come into their council. My glory, don’t be united to their assembly; for in their anger they killed men. In their self-will they hamstrung cattle.

Kikuyu: Marakara mao mahiũ mũno, na mangʼũrĩ mao matarĩ tha, maronyiitwo nĩ kĩrumi. Ngaamaharagania thĩinĩ wa Jakubu na ndĩmahurunjĩre thĩinĩ wa Isiraeli.
English: Cursed be their anger, for it was fierce; and their wrath, for it was cruel. I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel.

Kikuyu: “Juda, ariũ a thoguo nĩmagagũkumia; nakuo guoko gwaku nĩgũgatooria thũ ciaku; ariũ a thoguo nĩmagakũinamĩrĩra.
English: “Judah, your brothers will praise you. Your hand will be on the neck of your enemies. Your father’s sons will bow down before you.

Kikuyu: Juda, we ũrĩ kĩana kĩa mũrũũthi, mũrũ wakwa; we ũcookaga kuuma ũguĩmi-inĩ ũhũũnĩte ũguĩmi waku. O ta mũrũũthi-rĩ, ethunaga na agakoma thĩ, o ta mũrũũthi wa mũgoma-rĩ, nũũ ũngĩthĩgĩrĩria kũmwarahũra?
English: Judah is a lion’s cub. From the prey, my son, you have gone up. He stooped down, he crouched as a lion, as a lioness. Who will rouse him up?

Kikuyu: Rũthanju rwa ũthamaki rũtikoima harĩ Juda, o na kana rũthanju rwa ũnene rũthenge gatagatĩ-inĩ ka magũrũ make, o nginya agooka ũrĩa mwene ruo, o we ũrĩa ũgaathĩkagĩrwo nĩ thĩ.
English: The scepter will not depart from Judah, nor the ruler’s staff from between his feet, until he comes to whom it belongs. The obedience of the peoples will be to him.

Kikuyu: Akoohagĩrĩra ndigiri yake mũthabibũ-inĩ, nayo njaũ yake ya ndigiri amĩohagĩrĩre rũhonge-inĩ rũrĩa rwega mũno. Agaathambagia nguo ciake na ndibei, na kanjũ ciake na ũtune wa thabibũ.
English: Binding his foal to the vine, his donkey’s colt to the choice vine, he has washed his garments in wine, his robes in the blood of grapes.

Kikuyu: Maitho make makaira gũkĩra ndibei, namo magego make merũhe gũkĩra iria.
English: His eyes will be red with wine, his teeth white with milk.

Kikuyu: “Zebuluni agaatũũra hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria, na atuĩke handũ hega ha gũikaragwo nĩ marikabu; mĩhaka yake ĩgaakinya o Sidoni.
English: “Zebulun will dwell at the haven of the sea. He will be for a haven of ships. His border will be on Sidon.

Kikuyu: “Isakaru nĩ ndigiri ĩrĩa ĩrĩ hinya ĩkomete gatagatĩ ka mĩrigo ĩĩrĩ.
English: “Issachar is a strong donkey, lying down between the saddlebags.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa akoona ũrĩa ũhurũko wake ũrĩ mwega, na ũrĩa bũrũri wake wagĩrĩte-rĩ, no rĩo akainamĩrĩria kĩande gĩake akuue mĩrigo, na etĩkĩre kũruta wĩra ũtarĩ mũcaara.
English: He saw a resting place, that it was good, the land, that it was pleasant. He bows his shoulder to the burden, and becomes a servant doing forced labor.

Kikuyu: “Dani agaaciirĩrĩra andũ ake na kĩhooto, arĩ ta mũhĩrĩga ũmwe wa Isiraeli.
English: “Dan will judge his people, as one of the tribes of Israel.

Kikuyu: Dani agaatuĩka ta nyoka ĩrĩ mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra, atuĩke ta nduĩra ĩrĩ gacĩra-inĩ, ĩrĩa ĩrũmaga ndiira cia mbarathi, na tondũ ũcio mũmĩtwarithia akagũa na ngara.
English: Dan will be a serpent on the trail, an adder in the path, that bites the horse’s heels, so that his rider falls backward.

Kikuyu: “Wee Jehova-rĩ, njetereire kũhonokania gwaku.
English: I have waited for your salvation, Yahweh.

Kikuyu: “Gadi nĩagatharĩkĩrwo nĩ ikundi cia atunyani, nowe nĩagacitharĩkĩra aciingatithie.
English: “A troop will press on Gad, but he will press on their heel.

Kikuyu: “Irio cia Asheri nĩ cia kũguna mwĩrĩ; na akaaheanaga irio njega ingĩkenia mũthamaki.
English: “Asher’s food will be rich. He will produce royal dainties.

Kikuyu: “Nafitali nĩ ta thiiya ya mũgoma ĩrekereirio, ĩrĩa ĩciaraga tũthiiya tũthaka.
English: “Naphtali is a doe set free, who bears beautiful fawns.

Kikuyu: “Jusufu nĩ mũthabibũ ũrĩa ũciaraga, mũthabibũ ũrĩa ũciaraga ũrĩ hakuhĩ na gĩthima, ũrĩa honge ciaguo itambagĩrĩra rũthingo.
English: “Joseph is a fruitful vine, a fruitful vine by a spring. His branches run over the wall.

Kikuyu: Aikia a mĩguĩ nĩmamũtharĩkĩire marĩ na marũrũ; makĩmũikĩria mĩguĩ marĩ na ũũru mũingĩ.
English: The archers have severely grieved him, shot at him, and persecuted him:

Kikuyu: No ũta wake ndwenyenyekire, moko make marĩ hinya magĩikara merũmĩtie nĩ ũndũ wa guoko kwa Mwene-Hinya wa Jakubu, na nĩ ũndũ wa Mũrĩithi, o we Ihiga rĩa Isiraeli,
English: But his bow remained strong. The arms of his hands were made strong, by the hands of the Mighty One of Jacob, (from there is the shepherd, the stone of Israel),

Kikuyu: nĩ ũndũ wa Ngai wa thoguo, ũrĩa ũgũteithagia, na tondũ wa Mwene-Hinya-Wothe, ũrĩa ũkũrathimaga na irathimo cia igũrũ, na irathimo cia kũrĩa kũriku mũhuro wa thĩ, o na irathimo cia nyondo na cia nda.
English: even by the God of your father, who will help you, by the Almighty, who will bless you, with blessings of heaven above, blessings of the deep that lies below, blessings of the breasts, and of the womb.

Kikuyu: Irathimo cia thoguo nĩ makĩria ma irathimo cia irĩma iria cia tene, na nĩ makĩria ma ũtonga wa tũrĩma tũrĩa tũkũrũ. Ici ciothe nĩirekwo ihurũke mũtwe-inĩ wa Jusufu, o thiithi-inĩ wa ũcio ũhaana ta mwana wa mũthamaki harĩ ariũ a ithe.
English: The blessings of your father have prevailed above the blessings of my ancestors, above the boundaries of the ancient hills. They will be on the head of Joseph, on the crown of the head of him who is separated from his brothers.

Kikuyu: “Benjamini ahaana ta njũũi ĩrĩa ngoroku; kĩroko-inĩ atambuuraga kĩrĩa aguĩmĩte, hwaĩ-inĩ akagayania kĩrĩa atahĩte.”
English: “Benjamin is a ravenous wolf. In the morning he will devour the prey. At evening he will divide the plunder.”

Kikuyu: Ĩyo yothe nĩyo mĩhĩrĩga ĩrĩa ikũmi na ĩĩrĩ ya Isiraeli, na ũguo nĩguo ithe wao aameerire rĩrĩa aamarathimaga, akĩhe o mũndũ kĩrathimo kĩrĩa kĩamwagĩrĩire.
English: All these are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father spoke to them, and blessed them. He blessed everyone according to his own blessing.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jakubu akĩmahe mawatho maya: “Ndĩ hakuhĩ gũkua. Mũgaathika harĩa maithe makwa mathikĩtwo, ngurunga-inĩ ĩrĩa ĩrĩ gĩthaka-inĩ kĩa Efironi ũrĩa Mũhiti,
English: He instructed them, and said to them, “I am to be gathered to my people. Bury me with my fathers in the cave that is in the field of Ephron the Hittite,

Kikuyu: ngurunga ĩrĩa ĩrĩ gĩthaka- inĩ kĩa Makipela hakuhĩ na Mamure kũu Kaanani, ĩrĩa Iburahĩmu aagũranĩirie na gĩthaka kĩu, ĩrĩ handũ ha gũthikana, kuuma kũrĩ Efironi ũrĩa Mũhiti.
English: in the cave that is in the field of Machpelah, which is before Mamre, in the land of Canaan, which Abraham bought with the field from Ephron the Hittite as a burial place.

Kikuyu: Hau nĩho Iburahĩmu na mũtumia wake Sara maathikirwo, na no ho Isaaka na Rebeka mũtumia wake maathikirwo, na no ho ndaathikire Lea.
English: There they buried Abraham and Sarah, his wife. There they buried Isaac and Rebekah, his wife, and there I buried Leah:

Kikuyu: Gĩthaka kĩu na ngurunga ĩyo ciagũrirwo kuuma kũrĩ Ahethi.”
English: the field and the cave that is therein, which was purchased from the children of Heth.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jakubu aarĩkia kũhe ariũ ake ũhoro ũcio-rĩ, agĩthuna magũrũ make arĩ o gĩtanda-inĩ, agĩtuĩkana, agĩcookanĩrĩrio kũrĩ andũ ao arĩa maakuĩte. 50
English: When Jacob finished charging his sons, he gathered up his feet into the bed, breathed his last breath, and was gathered to his people. 50

Kikuyu: Nake Jusufu agĩkĩĩgũithia thĩ mbere ya ithe, akĩmũrĩrĩra na akĩmũmumunya.
English: Joseph fell on his father’s face, wept on him, and kissed him.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ agĩcooka agĩatha athondekani arĩa maarĩ ndungata ciake mathondeke mwĩrĩ wa ithe Isiraeli nĩguo ndũkabuthe. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio athondekani makĩmũthondeka,
English: Joseph commanded his servants, the physicians, to embalm his father; and the physicians embalmed Israel.

Kikuyu: handũ ha ihinda rĩa thikũ mĩrongo ĩna, tondũ rĩu nĩrĩo ihinda rĩrĩa rĩabatarainie rĩa kũũthondeka. Nao andũ a Misiri makĩmũcakaĩra matukũ mĩrongo mũgwanja.
English: Forty days were used for him, for that is how many days it takes to embalm. The Egyptians wept for Israel for seventy days.

Kikuyu: Namo matukũ ma kũmũcakaĩra maathira-rĩ, Jusufu akĩĩra kĩama kĩa Firaũni atĩrĩ, “Angĩkorwo nĩnjĩtĩkĩrĩkĩte maitho-inĩ manyu-rĩ, njarĩrĩriai kũrĩ Firaũni. Mwĩrei atĩrĩ,
English: When the days of weeping for him were past, Joseph spoke to Pharaoh’s staff, saying, “If now I have found favor in your eyes, please speak in the ears of Pharaoh, saying,

Kikuyu: ‘Baba nĩatũmire ndĩĩhĩte na mwĩhĩtwa, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ndĩ hakuhĩ gũkua; na ndakua ũgaathika mbĩrĩra-inĩ ĩrĩa niĩ mwene ndeyenjeire bũrũri-inĩ wa Kaanani.” Rĩu rekei nyambate thiĩ ngathike baba; na nĩngũcooka hũndũke.’ ”
English: ‘My father made me swear, saying, “Behold, I am dying. Bury me in my grave which I have dug for myself in the land of Canaan.” Now therefore, please let me go up and bury my father, and I will come again.’ ”

Kikuyu: Nake Firaũni akĩmũtũmanĩra, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ambata, ũthiĩ ũgathike thoguo, o ta ũrĩa aatũmire wĩhĩte.”
English: Pharaoh said, “Go up, and bury your father, just like he made you swear.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jusufu akĩambata agĩthiĩ gũthika ithe. Nao anene othe a Firaũni magĩthiĩ nake, andũ a gĩtĩĩo othe a kĩama kĩa Firaũni o na andũ arĩa atĩĩku a Misiri othe,
English: Joseph went up to bury his father; and with him went up all the servants of Pharaoh, the elders of his house, all the elders of the land of Egypt,

Kikuyu: o hamwe na andũ othe a nyũmba ya Jusufu, na ariũ a ithe, na arĩa othe maarĩ a nyũmba ya ithe. No ciana ciki na ndũũru ciao cia mbũri na cia ngʼombe ciatigĩtwo Gosheni.
English: all the house of Joseph, his brothers, and his father’s house. Only their little ones, their flocks, and their herds, they left in the land of Goshen.

Kikuyu: Thigari cia ngaari cia ita na cia mbarathi o na cio igĩthiĩ nake. Kĩarĩ gĩkundi kĩnene mũno kĩa andũ.
English: Both chariots and horsemen went up with him. It was a very great company.

Kikuyu: Nao maakinya ihuhĩro-inĩ rĩa ngano rĩa Atadi, hakuhĩ na Jorodani, makĩrĩra manĩrĩire marĩ na ruo; na hau Jusufu agĩcakaĩra ithe matukũ mũgwanja.
English: They came to the threshing floor of Atad, which is beyond the Jordan, and there they lamented with a very great and severe lamentation. He mourned for his father seven days.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Akaanani arĩa maatũũraga kũu moonire macakaya macio hau ihuhĩro-inĩ rĩa ngano rĩa Atadi, makĩĩrana atĩrĩ, “Andũ a Misiri marĩ na igongona rĩa macakaya ma kĩeha.” Na nĩkĩo handũ hau hakuhĩ na Jorodani heetagwo Abeli- Miziraimu.
English: When the inhabitants of the land, the Canaanites, saw the mourning in the floor of Atad, they said, “This is a grievous mourning by the Egyptians.” Therefore its name was called Abel Mizraim, which is beyond the Jordan.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ariũ a Jakubu magĩĩka o ũrĩa aamaathĩte:
English: His sons did to him just as he commanded them,

Kikuyu: Makĩmũkuua makĩmũtwara bũrũri wa Kaanani, na makĩmũthika ngurunga-inĩ ĩrĩa yarĩ gĩthaka-inĩ kĩa Makipela, hakuhĩ na Mamure, ĩrĩa Iburahĩmu aagũranĩirie na gĩthaka kĩu kĩrĩ handũ ha gũthikanwo kuuma kũrĩ Efironi ũrĩa Mũhiti.
English: for his sons carried him into the land of Canaan, and buried him in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which Abraham bought with the field, as a possession for a burial site, from Ephron the Hittite, near Mamre.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa gũthika ithe-rĩ, Jusufu agĩcooka Misiri hamwe na ariũ a ithe na arĩa othe maathiĩte nake gũthika ithe.
English: Joseph returned into Egypt—he, and his brothers, and all that went up with him to bury his father, after he had buried his father.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa ariũ a ithe moonire atĩ ithe wao nĩakua, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ĩ angĩkorwo Jusufu nĩatũũire atũiguagĩra ũũru, na atũrĩhie maũru marĩa mothe twamwĩkire?”
English: When Joseph’s brothers saw that their father was dead, they said, “It may be that Joseph will hate us, and will fully pay us back for all the evil which we did to him.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio magĩtũmana kũrĩ Jusufu makiuga atĩrĩ, “Thoguo atanakua nĩatigire oiga atĩrĩ:
English: They sent a message to Joseph, saying, “Your father commanded before he died, saying,

Kikuyu: ‘Ũũ nĩguo mũkeera Jusufu: ndagũthaitha ũrekere ariũ a thoguo mehia na mahĩtia marĩa meekire nĩ ũndũ nĩmagwĩkire ũũru mũno.’ Na rĩu twagũthaitha wohere ndungata cia Ngai wa thoguo mehia macio.” Na rĩrĩa ndũmĩrĩri yao yamũkinyĩire, Jusufu akĩrĩra.
English: ‘You shall tell Joseph, “Now please forgive the disobedience of your brothers, and their sin, because they did evil to you.” ’ Now, please forgive the disobedience of the servants of the God of your father.” Joseph wept when they spoke to him.

Kikuyu: Ariũ a ithe magĩcooka magĩthiĩ kũrĩ we, makĩĩgũithia thĩ mbere yake, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Tũrĩ ngombo ciaku.”
English: His brothers also went and fell down before his face; and they said, “Behold, we are your servants.”

Kikuyu: No Jusufu akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Tigai gwĩtigĩra. Kaĩ niĩ akĩrĩ niĩ Ngai?
English: Joseph said to them, “Don’t be afraid, for am I in the place of God?

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ mwaciirĩte kũnjĩka ũũru, no Ngai aanjiirĩire ũndũ mwega nĩguo akinyanĩrie ũrĩa kũrekĩka rĩu, ũhoro wa kũhonokia mĩoyo ya andũ aingĩ.
English: As for you, you meant evil against me, but God meant it for good, to save many people alive, as is happening today.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio-rĩ, mũtigetigĩre. Nĩndĩĩmũheaga irio cianyu na cia ciana cianyu.” Nake akĩmomĩrĩria na akĩmarĩria wega.
English: Now therefore don’t be afraid. I will provide for you and your little ones.” He comforted them, and spoke kindly to them.

Kikuyu: Jusufu agĩikara Misiri, hamwe na andũ othe a nyũmba ya ithe. Aatũũrire muoyo mĩaka igana rĩmwe na ikũmi,
English: Joseph lived in Egypt, he, and his father’s house. Joseph lived one hundred ten years.

Kikuyu: na akĩona rũciaro rwa gatatũ rwa ciana cia Efiraimu. O na ciana cia Makiru mũrũ wa Manase ciaciarwo nĩciaigĩrĩirwo maru-inĩ ma Jusufu.
English: Joseph saw Ephraim’s children to the third generation. The children also of Machir, the son of Manasseh, were born on Joseph’s knees.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jusufu akĩĩra ariũ a ithe atĩrĩ, “Ndĩ hakuhĩ gũkua. No rĩrĩ, ti-itherũ Ngai nĩagooka kũmũrora amũteithie na amũrute kuuma bũrũri ũyũ, amũtware bũrũri ũrĩa erĩire Iburahĩmu, na Isaaka na Jakubu na mwĩhĩtwa.”
English: Joseph said to his brothers, “I am dying, but God will surely visit you, and bring you up out of this land to the land which he swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jusufu agĩtũma ariũ a Isiraeli mehĩte na mwĩhĩtwa, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ Ngai nĩagooka kũmũteithia, na hĩndĩ ĩyo yakinya no nginya mũgaakuua mahĩndĩ makwa kuuma kũndũ gũkũ.”
English: Joseph took an oath from the children of Israel, saying, “God will surely visit you, and you shall carry up my bones from here.”

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Jusufu agĩkua, arĩ na ũkũrũ wa mĩaka igana rĩmwe na ikũmi. Thuutha wa kũmũthondeka ndakabuthe, makĩmũiga ithandũkũ-inĩ rĩa gũthikwo narĩo o kũu Misiri.
English: So Joseph died, being one hundred ten years old, and they embalmed him, and he was put in a coffin in Egypt.
